  • fiscal-meta-indices.test.ts — pins the STALE-POSITIONAL-INDEX bug (§7): re-resolves every fiscal bloc's fiscal_meta.json indices by variable id and asserts the derived debt path stays bounded (the EA-equity-index explosion guard); also pins the level-display drag conversion.
  • gvar-convergence.test.ts — Gauss–Seidel ≡ direct solver to ~1e-11; reports ρ(M). (fast)
  • gvar-closed-form-routing.test.ts — closed-form (I−M)⁻¹d ≡ iterative on a US-endogenous fixture; cache-hit ≡ cold rebuild; a pin-structure change (US OR RoW) → new M. (heavy)
  • gvar-us-endogenous.test.ts — the augmented-state endogenous solve (RoW→US star feedback + US→RoW global channel) ≡ the iterative fixed point. (heavy)
  • gvar-direct-channel.test.ts / gvar-kernel-affine-independence.test.ts — solveGVARDirect is the one-hop preview; the affine kernels are value-invariant (byte-equal to the probe loop). (heavy)
  • gvar-globals.test.ts — the global-common pins (own-base + US delta; zero spillover at zero scenario).
  • gvar-coupling-artifact.test.ts — the pre-shipped (gzipped) coupling_default.json.gz hydrates and matches a fresh build. (fast)
  • gvar-derived-topology.test.ts — configureGVARTopology/deriveBlocIdx resolve indices + US_ENDOGENOUS from spec+weights.
  • gvar-band-solve.test.ts — the per-draw (experimental) band centres on the propagated path. (heavy)
  • gvar-drag-scheduling.test.ts / gvar-drag-line-band.test.ts — the drag-perf boundary: no GVAR solve fires mid-drag; one direct solve on pointer-up; the dragged line falls back to condForecast while dragging (§5a/§8b).
  • chartgrid-render-memo.test.ts — one conditioning edit rebuilds ≈1 of ~35 panels (§3a).
  • conditioned-dot-optimistic.test.ts / right-click-plant-at-cursor.test.ts — the optimistic dot round-trips to the cursor (conditionedDotY.ts inverse pair); right-click plants the bullet (§8b).
  • gvar-ui-linkage.test.ts — the dropdown/selector GVAR membership tracks isGvarCountry.
  • longRunTilt.test.ts — the entropic tilt hits the target long-run moment.

docs.title: 12. Build, gate, deploy
docs.text:
cd app
npx tsc --noEmit                       # types
npm test                               # fast suite (excludes the heavy solver canaries)
npm run test:heavy                     # heavy solver / perf-parity canaries (1 fork)
BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario npm run build # static export with the deploy base path

Green gate = tsc 0 + npm test green + npm run test:heavy green + build succeeds, before any deploy. (npm test is vitest run minus the heavy --exclude globs; npm run test:heavy runs exactly those. Both halves are mandatory — §11.)

Deploy path: push to the bvarscenario git remote (matyasfarkas/BVARScenarioTool) main, touching app/**. Its GitHub Actions workflow (deploy-to-website.yml) builds the static export (BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario) and copies app/out/* into the matyasfarkas/starter-hugo-academic website repo's static/bvar-scenario/. That repo is a Hugo Academic site published to GitHub Pages (note the netlify.toml trigger), which rebuilds and serves the tool at the /bvar-scenario path. This workflow has no Hugo step; any Hugo pin (0.122.0 — Hugo 0.123+ removed getCSV and broke the wowchemy theme, so do not bump it) lives in the starter-hugo-academic repo, not here. The origin remote (matyasfarkas/BVAR-Policy-Calibration-Tool) is the source repo, not the deploy target.

Commit only deploy-relevant files; leave pipeline/ scratch (diagnostics, staging, raw CSVs) untracked. Never commit app/.env.local / FRED_API_KEY.

docs.title: 12a. Re-estimation + coupling-artifact runbook (the data refresh path)
docs.text:
The models are estimated offline and ported into app/public/data/bvar. Verify each command against the actual scripts before running — do not invent flags.

  1. Estimate — pipeline/export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m is the GVAR-EM iterative estimator: it runs the 33 blocs in parallel (parfor), estimating each by cgl_bvarV5([domestic | 3 stars]) with the DK smoother filling the missing star/COVID quarters (COVID-as-missing), and iterates the stars to the Monte-Carlo noise floor (env MAXIT iterations, TOL; MAXIT=1 ⇒ single-country/separated). It reads the per-bloc raw pipeline/country_rawdata/DataRaw_<CC>_glob.json. Set env US_ENDOGENOUS=1 (requires a US row in the weights file) to estimate the US as a coupled bloc — this is what produces the deployed US/spec.json with its 3 ROW*_US stars. Key cgl_bvarV5 flags: sur=0, noc=1, MNpsi=0 (mirror them exactly when porting). The estimator also reads the per-variable prior from the spec/raw and builds the stationary-column set pos = find(prior=='WN'), passed as the 3rd positional argument of cgl_bvarV5 (the WN own-lag-0 prior on the fiscal GDP-ratios — §6a); it is empty (a no-op) for the 44 non-fiscal blocs.
  2. Fiscal — pipeline/build_fiscal_<cc>_4var.py (one per US/EA/JP/UK/CA/CN) builds the 4-driver fiscal block {FREV_GDP, FPEXP_GDP, EFFI, SFA}, where SFA is computed as the exact residual that closes the app debt recursion (COVID quarters → NaN). Writes pipeline/fiscal_meta/<CC>.json ({d0, sfaDefault, idx:{gdp,deflator,revenue,primaryExp,effRate,sfa}}).
  3. Port — copy all 33 blocs into app/public/data/bvar/<CC>/ (6-significant-figure JSON) and refresh each fiscal bloc's fiscal_meta.json. Then run node pipeline/fix_fiscal_meta_indices.mjs — the porter does NOT regenerate the positional fiscal indices, so a variable-order change silently re-points them (the EA-debt-explosion bug; §7 STALE-POSITIONAL-INDEX gotcha). The script re-resolves all six indices by variable id from the deployed spec.json (idempotent); fiscal-meta-indices.test.ts then catches any that are still stale.
  4. Regenerate the coupling artifact (MANDATORY after any data change — modelSig keys it to the data): from app/, npx vite-node scripts/precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs. It builds M in JS (buildGVARCouplingMEndo when US_ENDOGENOUS, else buildGVARCouplingM) for the empty pin structure, computes the inverse via MATLAB BLAS (invert_coupling.m, shelled matlab -batch — the in-JS O(dim³) inverse at dim≈2060 is ~46 min), and writes app/public/data/bvar/gvar/coupling_default.json.gz (gzip, 6-sigfig: {modelSig, pinStructure:'', dim, rhoSpectral, M, invImM}). gvar-coupling-artifact.test.ts asserts the artifact matches a fresh build.
  5. Gate + deploy — green gate (above), then the deploy path (below). Push to the bvarscenario remote → GitHub Action copies app/out into starter-hugo-academic → Hugo rebuilds & serves the tool at the /bvar-scenario path on GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 13. Conventions to respect (a checklist before you commit)
docs.text:
1. Don't hardcode variable indices. Read n from companion_mean.json, fiscal indices from fiscal_meta.json, GVAR indices from IDX.
  2. Respect the data space. A stray ×4 or /100 between level and growth space will diverge forecasts silently — the canary tests catch it.
  3. The GVAR bloc set is data-driven (33 blocs: endogenous US + EA + 31 RoW), not a hardcoded list. Never hardcode a subset like ['US','EA','JP'] — read ROW_COUNTRIES / COUPLED_COUNTRIES / IDX (live, set by configureGVARTopology) or iterate the active candidates from gvarLoader.ts. Note the US is now in the fixed point (COUPLED_COUNTRIES, gated by US_ENDOGENOUS): US pins are NOT free — they change the US smoother and thus M, so they enter the coupling-cache key and the pin-structure signature. A leftover pilot subset loop in the band request builder once silently dropped some blocs' own-pins (band on baseline while the line followed the propagated path); that class of regression is invisible to tsc and only shows on the dropped blocs.
  4. gvarPins persists across setCountry — by design.
  5. Exact MATLAB replication when porting an estimator — no shortcuts; flag and ask if unsure.
  6. The green gate is mandatory before deploy — and it is two halves (npm test + npm run test:heavy), not one vitest run. See §11.

docs.title: 13a. Known issues & limitations (flagged-for-fix, NOT yet fixed)
docs.text:
These are flagged-but-unfixed at the current deployment — document them honestly; do not treat the affected output as verified. The module-reference MODULES.md § Known issues carries the file:line detail; the short forms:

  1. CN fiscal debt — inflation double-count (RESOLVED 2026-06). The CN debt roll-forward used to read Δ(GDP) + Δ(deflator) against an already-NOMINAL GDP_CN, double-counting inflation. The surgical CN-only re-estimate replaced GDP_CN with real GDP (spec.json var 0 = "CN Real GDP …", fiscal_meta.json idx.gdpIsNominal = false), so the growth term is now the correct real-GDP + deflator nominal growth, matching US/EA/JP/UK/CA. The fix is CN-only; the other 32 blocs are byte-identical.
  2. Entropic long-run tilt does not filter non-finite moments. solveEntropicTilt feeds the raw per-draw long-run moments into the Newton dual; a single non-finite moment from an unstable draw NaN-poisons the softmax weights and silently empties the anchor band. Fix: drop non-finite draws before the iteration (as the band-step weightedQuantile already does). (longRunTilt.ts.)
  3. No ρ(M) < 1 divergence guard on the live closed-form solve. The endogenous Neumann fixed point iterates to maxIt/tolerance with no stationarity check: a refit yielding ρ(M) ≥ 1 would return a confident-wrong forecast with no warning. ρ is shipped in the artifact (currently 0.924) but not read as a live guard. (gvarSolve.ts:1296.)
  4. Conditional band uses the FILTERED, not DK-smoothed, covariance. conditionalForecast returns the smoothed MEAN but the FILTERED variance, so the band is overstated for quarters that precede a future pin (the smoother would tighten them). Mean correct; band conservative. (kalman.ts:405.)
  5. The porter does not regenerate fiscal_meta.json (STALE-POSITIONAL-INDEX). Positional indices go stale on any variable-order change (the EA/UK debt-explosion cause). Mandatory mitigation: run node pipeline/fix_fiscal_meta_indices.mjs after every re-port; fiscal-meta-indices.test.ts guards it. (§7, port_gvar_exports.py.)

docs.title: 14. Empirical evaluation — lives in a SEPARATE repo (em-bayesian-gvar-eval)
docs.text:
The forecast-evaluation / publication work is not in this repository. A dedicated harness repo, /home/farkas/github/em-bayesian-gvar-eval, implements the paper's two empirical cores. It references this repo, never copies it — its drivers addpath into $BVAR_TOOL_ROOT (default "/home/farkas/github/BVAR Policy Calibration Tool") and re-use cgl_bvarV5.m, export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m, the per-bloc raw panels, the trade weights, and gvarSolve.ts directly. There are NO copies of the estimator or solver in the eval repo, and there is NO eval / horse-race / scoring code in this repo (verified: nothing under this repo's git ls-files matches the harness). The full, code-grounded design is em-bayesian-gvar-eval/docs/EMPIRICAL_PROTOCOL.md — read it before touching the evaluation.

⚠️ The DESIGN is final and a first full scoring pass has now been produced (em-bayesian-gvar-eval/results/tables.{txt,json}, results/tierA/us_rate_100bp.json). The headline read below is from those files (56 origins 2010Q1–2024Q4 with 2020 dropped, 9 models). Treat the numbers as a first run with the honest caveats stated — CRPS + MCS are the robust primaries; the parametric log-score column is numerically broken and the draw-based densities are over-confident.

The two cores:

  1. Out-of-sample forecast horse-race (Part 1). Recursive expanding-window, origins 2010Q1–2024Q4 (drop the 2020 COVID origins for the headline), leak-free ROLLING as-of-t0 trade weights (rebuilt from IMTS bilateral trade ≤ t0 — using the shipped 2021–2023 weights at an early origin would inject future trade structure), targets {real GDP growth, inflation, short rate} for US/EA/JP/UK/CA/CN, horizons h ∈ {1,2,4,8}. COVID-as-missing is re-applied in-sample at every origin whose window spans 2020Q2–Q4.

  2. Endogenous-US spillover analysis (Part 2). The endogenous-vs-frozen conditional-scenario contrast that this repo's gvarSolve.ts already computes is Tier A (the headline, zero new code: solveGVARClosedFormEndo vs the frozen path). GIRF / FEVD / Diebold–Yilmaz connectedness is Tier B — new code in the eval repo (constructible from the exported betahat/Σ + the coupling M), not a call into the shipped solver. The shipped solver computes conditional-mean scenario deltas, NOT GIRFs/FEVD — do not relabel a multi-period rate pin a "shock" or a "GIRF."

Models in the race (config ids — em-bayesian-gvar-eval/config/eval_config.yaml):

id Model What it is emgvar_endo Proposed, endogenous US the full §5·EM loop, US_ENDOGENOUS=1 emgvar_frozen Proposed, frozen anchor US estimated once as a closed anchor; cheaper, and the Part-2 contrast rw / rw_clim Random walk / climatology driftless no-change (headline reference) + sample-mean anchor arp AR(p) per series p by AIC over {1..4}; iterated + direct sbvar Single-country BVAR strip the 3 ROW* stars, identical cgl_bvarV5 (value of coupling) cgvar_a Textbook OLS GVAR Pesaran–Schuermann–Weiner / Dees-et-al VARX*, fixed weights, no COVID-as-missing (the literature competitor) cgvar_b Single-pass Bayesian GVAR MAXIT=1, GLP + COVID-as-missing (the clean EM ablation) dfm Dynamic factor model 3–5 factors, Bayesian VAR on factors (non-GVAR multivariate benchmark)

Scoring: CRPS (primary density score) + parametric log-score + PIT/coverage; significance via Giacomini–White (headline, valid under nesting + the expanding scheme), Clark–West (nested point), Diebold–Mariano (non-nested), with Romano–Wolf + Model Confidence Set for multiplicity.

Headline result (first full pass — 56 origins, 9 models; from results/tables.{txt,json}). AR(p) and the DFM have the lowest point CRPS, BUT the 90% Model Confidence Set tells the publishable story: emgvar_endo is IN the INFLATION MCS at every horizon (h=1,2,4,8) and IN the SHORT-RATE MCS at h=1,2,4 — statistically indistinguishable from the best on the nominal block — while it is OUT of the GDP-GROWTH MCS at h=1 and h=8. emgvar_endo ⪰ emgvar_frozen across the MCS (the endogenous-US design earns its keep — e.g. at RATE h=1 and GROWTH h=4 the endogenous bloc is in the set and the frozen one is removed). The EM-Bayesian treatment decisively beats the textbook OLS classical GVAR: cgvar_a is removed from most MCS sets (GROWTH h=1/4/8, INFL h=4/8, RATE h=8) and carries CRPS/RW up to ~2.5. Best inflation h=1 is emgvar_endo (relRMSE 0.799, CRPS/RW 0.814).

Honest caveats (must state, verified in the tables). - Draw-based densities are OVER-CONFIDENT. PIT cov90 for emgvar_endo runs ~0.73–0.83 (vs the 0.90 target); the frozen variant is similar. The bands are too tight at the draw counts used. - The parametric log-score column is numerically broken on the under-covered clouds (the LPS(G) entries blow up to ~1e14+). CRPS and the MCS are the robust primaries; treat LPS(G) as unusable.

Tier-A spillover (eval/tier_a_scenario.mjs, results/tierA/us_rate_100bp.json). A US +100 bp conditional projection shows non-trivial endogenous-only cross-country feedback that the frozen anchor structurally omits (the file's own meta.note: the endo−frozen gap is the RoW→US feedback the frozen anchor cannot represent). BUT the magnitudes are large and sign-flipping across horizons (e.g. the frozen-anchor US/UK growth paths swing to −4.4 / −3.1 at h=20), and it is a multi-period conditional projection, NOT a causal IRF (the file labels itself exactly that). The magnitudes need a validation pass before being headlined — do not over-claim a causal interpretation.

Naming note: the protocol references this repo's weights as weights_full.json (the 33-bloc, US-row file used for the eval pulls) and pipeline/country_rawdata/DataRaw_<CC>_glob.json; the app loads weights_24bloc.json (same 33-bloc content, stale name — §5). Both files exist here; do not assume they are identical byte-for-byte, but they carry the same bloc set.

docs.title: Build, Test & Deploy
docs.text:
How the EM-Bayesian Global VAR platform goes from a working tree to the live public site under the /bvar-scenario path of the academic website. This page documents the green gate (the local checks that must pass before any deploy), the test taxonomy (which suites block a deploy versus which guard a feature), and the deploy chain (the GitHub Action that builds the static export and republishes it into the starter-hugo-academic site repo, which publishes to GitHub Pages).

For the higher-level architecture and the data-refresh / re-estimation runbook, see ARCHITECTURE_FOR_AI.md and REESTIMATING_MODELS.md. This page does not repeat those.

docs.title: The green gate
docs.text:
Everything runs from the app/ directory. The commands below are the full pre-deploy contract — types clean, tests green (fast suite + the heavy solver canaries), static export succeeds — before any push to the deploy remote.

cd app
npx tsc --noEmit                       # 1. types: must report 0 errors
npm test                               # 2. fast suite (excludes the heavy solver canaries)
npm run test:heavy                     # 3. heavy solver/perf canaries (serialised, 1 fork)
BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario npm run build # 4. static export with the deploy base path
Step Command What it proves Types npx tsc --noEmit TypeScript compiles with no errors (no emit; type-check only). Fast tests npm test The fast compute suite in app/src/__tests__/compute/, minus the heavy solver canaries (see the split below), including the cheap canaries. Heavy tests npm run test:heavy The expensive full-panel solver / affine-kernel / band-solve canaries, run serialised (--poolOptions.forks.maxForks=1) so the multi-second US-endogenous solves do not contend for cores. Build npm run build (next build) The Next.js static export (output: 'export') completes and writes app/out/.

Green gate = tsc 0 errors and npm test green and npm run test:heavy green and the build succeeds. Only then is a deploy allowed. npx vitest run (= npm run test:all) runs everything in one pass — use it for a full sweep, but note it runs the heavy suites with default parallelism (slower, can over-subscribe).

The fast/heavy split is real, and it is in package.json — read it, don't assume. npm test is not vitest run: it is vitest run with a list of --exclude globs. The excluded (heavy) patterns are gvar-us-endogenous, gvar-direct-channel, gvar-band-solve, gvar-closed-form-*, gvar-band-closedform-*, affine-blockernels-parity, gvar-kernel-affine-independence. npm run test:heavy runs exactly those (matched by vitest run <name…>), one fork at a time. A change that only npm test covers leaves the dim-2060 solver paths unverified — both halves are mandatory before deploy. (npm run test:watch is vitest — the interactive watcher, never a CI gate.)

A few notes that are easy to get wrong:

  • The build is a static export: next.config.mjs sets output: 'export', images.unoptimized: true, and routes the deploy prefix through basePath/assetPrefix/NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH, all driven by the BASE_PATH env var (see BASE_PATH below).
  • This is not stock Next.js — see app/AGENTS.md. Read the bundled docs under node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before changing build config.

docs.title: Test taxonomy
docs.text:
All compute tests live in app/src/__tests__/compute/. They fall into three roles:

  • CANARY — guards a load-bearing invariant or a deployed artifact. A red canary means the shipped model data, the scaling convention, or the cross-country solver identity is broken; it must block the deploy, not be skipped.
  • FEATURE — proves a specific user-facing capability (a Taylor rule, optimal policy, the fiscal debt recursion, US-endogenous coupling, the entropic long-run tilt, GVAR globals/topology, …).
  • REGRESSION — pins a previously-fixed bug so it cannot silently return (scaling differencing, WYSIWYG conditioning, counterfactual recovery, …).

docs.title: Fast (npm test) vs heavy (npm run test:heavy) — which is which
docs.text:
npm test excludes seven heavy patterns; npm run test:heavy runs exactly those, serialised to one fork. The three deploy-blocking canaries (scenario-scale-convention, gvar-convergence, gvar-coupling-artifact) are all in the fast suite. The heavy suite is the multi-second full-panel solver and the affine-kernel/coupling perf-parity gates:

Heavy suite (npm run test:heavy) Why it is heavy --exclude glob (from package.json) currently-present files ----------- -------- gvar-us-endogenous gvar-us-endogenous.test.ts gvar-direct-channel gvar-direct-channel.test.ts gvar-band-solve gvar-band-solve.test.ts gvar-closed-form-* gvar-closed-form-routing.test.ts gvar-band-closedform-* (no file at present) affine-blockernels-parity affine-blockernels-parity.test.ts gvar-kernel-affine-independence gvar-kernel-affine-independence.test.ts

The fast/heavy split is keyed on glob patterns, not a fixed file list: npm test --excludes the seven patterns above and npm run test:heavy selects gvar-us-endogenous gvar-direct-channel gvar-band-solve gvar-closed-form gvar-band-closedform affine-blockernels gvar-kernel-affine — so a new gvar-closed-form-* or gvar-band-closedform-* file is auto-routed to the heavy half. Everything else in app/src/__tests__/compute/ runs under npm test. Both halves gate the deploy — a heavy suite that only test:heavy runs still blocks a release if red.

docs.title: Canary suites (block deploy)
docs.text:
Test file Guards scenario-scale-convention.test.ts The conditional-display scaling convention: response paths for log vars are shown as 4·(Δlevel_t − Δlevel_{t−1}) (q/q-annualized rate deviation), not 4·levelGap_t (cumulative price-level gap). Pins the corrected convention against the MATLAB conforekf ground truth for the canonical Baa +100bp / 8q shock; a break would re-introduce the ~23× far-horizon overstatement. gvar-convergence.test.ts Gauss-Seidel ≡ closed-form: for the same US scenario the iterative solveGVAR (Gauss-Seidel sweeps, US frozen) and the linear solveGVARLinear (build M by unit-RoW probes, then (I−M)⁻¹ d / Smith doubling) must agree to ~machine precision per RoW country. This is the proof that the N-bloc linear-solver generalization is correct; also reports empirical and power-iteration ρ(M). gvar-coupling-artifact.test.ts The pre-shipped coupling artifact (public/data/bvar/gvar/coupling_default.json.gz) is FRESH: its modelSig matches the deployed models, the augmented empty-pin-structure dim is self-consistent with the live US-endogenous build, ρ ∈ (0,1), and an artifact-hydrated solve equals the live-built closed-form solve (≤1e-3, the 6-sig-fig rounding bound). Also asserts the route REFUSES the empty-structure artifact for a non-empty (e.g. US-pinned) request. Skips loudly if the .gz is missing rather than failing.

These three are exactly the suites that, if red, must stop a release — they cover the two things a static deploy cannot self-correct: the shipped artifacts (coupling_default.json.gz, the per-bloc model JSON) and the display/solver conventions the UI renders on top of them.

docs.title: Feature suites
docs.text: Test file Capability taylor-rule.test.ts The 9 Taylor-rule variants (taylor-rule.ts). barnichon-mesters.test.ts The Barnichon-Mesters optimal-policy optimizer. kalman.test.ts State-space build, Kalman filter/smoother, conditionalForecast (DK smoother). conditional-forecast.test.ts Conditional forecasting on real sample data. dynamic-conditioning.test.ts Any variable can be conditioned (general dynamic conditioning). linear-combo-conditioning.test.ts Conditioning on a linear combination (e.g. primary balance = revenue − primary expenditure) inside the smoother, without a separate BVAR variable. chart-data.test.ts Chart-data generation for all 8 display panels. fiscalAccounting.test.ts The fiscal debt recursion and exact SFA residual (fiscalAccounting.ts). fiscal-conditioning-integration.test.ts Conditioning on a debt/GDP trajectory: the engine inverts the recursion to the required primary balance and pins it via a linear constraint. gvar-globals.test.ts The 4 GVAR global commons (GLB_OIL/GLB_USLR/GLB_USBAA/GLB_USEQ) — US determines them as its own variables. gvar-derived-topology.test.ts Self-configuring bloc topology (ROW_COUNTRIES / IDX / W) DERIVED from deployed spec.json + weights_24bloc.json, not hardcoded. gvar-us-endogenous.test.ts The US-as-coupled-bloc solver generalization (its ROW*_US stars + US weights row). gvar-band-solve.test.ts Per-draw GVAR scenario band solve. gvar-ui-linkage.test.ts Wiring of the cross-country engine into the UI (buildScenarioPins, deviation → pinned LEVEL matrices). gvar-closed-form-routing.test.ts The production solve path: runGVARSolve routed through solveGVARClosedForm (assemble each bloc's affine RoW response once, build M, solve x = (I−M)⁻¹ d) + coupling-cache correctness. (heavy) gvar-direct-channel.test.ts The US-endogenous direct preview channel on a bloc subset. (heavy) longRunTilt.test.ts The entropic long-run tilt + PLR (longRunTilt.ts). chartgrid-render-memo.test.ts The Phase-2 render memoization (reconcileCharts + chartDeepEqual + React.memo(DraggableChart)): one conditioning edit rebuilds ≈1 of ~35 panels, not all of them (asserts via chartRebuildCounter/draggableChartRenderCounter). conditioned-dot-optimistic.test.ts The optimistic conditioned-point dot (conditionedDotDisplayY ∘ conditioningDeviationFromDisplayY = identity) — the marker round-trips to the clicked display value for every variable type. right-click-plant-at-cursor.test.ts Right-click on a forecast chart plants the bullet at the cursor (same display→stored mapping as drag). gvar-drag-scheduling.test.ts The drag-perf boundary: no GVAR mean solve fires while a drag is in progress; exactly one direct solve fires on pointer-up (via the gvarDragState bus + the 100 ms release macrotask). gvar-drag-line-band.test.ts While dragging a GVAR bloc, its line falls back to the cheap single-country condForecast and flips back to the propagated GVAR path on release.

docs.title: Regression suites
docs.text:
Test file Bug it pins conditioning-wysiwyg.test.ts A directly-conditioned variable must display exactly the dragged path (WYSIWYG), including sparse/partial per-quarter conditioning — the scaling-differencing fix must apply only to RESPONSE vars. counterfactual-irf.test.ts A counterfactual recovers the IRF when the rate adjustment equals the IRF. counterfactual-recovers-scenario.test.ts A counterfactual with a vanishing policy adjustment must recover the scenario (policy effect measured against the right baseline). fiscal-backcast.test.ts The budget-identity recursion in fiscalAccounting.ts reproduces the observed US federal debt/GDP back-cast from observed drivers (Step-1 gating). matrix.test.ts Core linear algebra (Cholesky/solve/inverse, ~1e-15 singularity threshold) in matrix.ts. affine-blockernels-parity.test.ts The affineBlocKernels perf path reproduces the old full filter+smoother probe loop to <1e-9 (baseline mean Sc and every per-period kernel) and is >=3× faster, on the real US-endogenous bloc — the optimization must stay a numerical no-op. (heavy) gvar-kernel-affine-independence.test.ts The affine-independence claim underlying the per-bloc kernel cache-key change (RANK-1 gate). (heavy) fiscal-meta-indices.test.ts Pins the STALE-POSITIONAL-INDEX bug (§ below): re-resolves every fiscal bloc's fiscal_meta.json indices by variable id from the deployed spec.json and asserts each bloc's derived debt path stays bounded — the regression that caught EA's effRate landing on the equity index (col ~537) so debt/GDP exploded. Also pins the level-display drag conversion (oil → $100 must not over-shoot to ~$190).

The perf-fix parity suites (affine-blockernels-parity, gvar-kernel-affine-independence) are regression gates in spirit — they prove an optimization is numerically a no-op — but because they protect the production GVAR solve path, treat a red result there with the same severity as a canary.

The STALE-POSITIONAL-INDEX gotcha (porter must regen). fiscal_meta.json stores the fiscal-driver column indices positionally, but the porter (pipeline/port_gvar_exports.py) does not regenerate them. Any variable-order change (the 33-bloc GVAR-EM refit inserted vars ahead of the fiscal block in every spec except US) silently re-points the indices at the wrong columns — the cause of the recent EA/UK/CA/JP/CN debt explosion. The fix is to run node pipeline/fix_fiscal_meta_indices.mjs (re-resolves the six indices by variable id from the deployed spec.json, preserves d0/sfaDefault, idempotent) after porting and before the gate; fiscal-meta-indices.test.ts is the canary-grade guard that blocks a deploy with stale indices. See REESTIMATING_MODELS.md and the DATA_SCHEMA fiscal-meta section.

The file list above is enumerated from app/src/__tests__/compute/. When you add a test, slot it into the right row here and confirm it actually runs under vitest run.

docs.title: Deploy chain
docs.text:
The source repo is matyasfarkas/BVAR-Policy-Calibration-Tool (the origin remote). The deploy target is the bvarscenario remote (matyasfarkas/BVARScenarioTool), whose GitHub Action builds the export and republishes it into the matyasfarkas/starter-hugo-academic website repo. That site is a Hugo Academic site published to GitHub Pages (note the netlify.toml trigger). This workflow contains no Hugo step: it only copies app/out/* into the website repo, and the Hugo build downstream then rebuilds and serves it.

push -> bvarscenario main
   │   (paths: app/** or netlify.toml)
   ▼
.github/workflows/deploy-to-website.yml   (runner: ubuntu-latest, Node 22)
   │  npm ci
   │  BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario npm run build      → app/out/
   │  checkout matyasfarkas/starter-hugo-academic
   │  rm -rf website/static/bvar-scenario/*
   │  cp -r app/out/* website/static/bvar-scenario/
   │  commit + push to the website repo
   ▼
Hugo rebuilds the academic (Hugo Academic) site from the website repo
   ▼
served at the /bvar-scenario path of the academic site (GitHub Pages)

Workflow facts, verified against .github/workflows/deploy-to-website.yml:

  • Triggers: push to main with changed paths under app/** or netlify.toml, plus manual workflow_dispatch.
  • Runner / toolchain: ubuntu-latest, actions/setup-node@v4 with node-version: 22 and npm cache keyed on app/package-lock.json.
  • Build step runs in working-directory: app: npm ci then npm run build, with BASE_PATH: /bvar-scenario in the environment.
  • Publish step checks out matyasfarkas/starter-hugo-academic (via secrets.WEBSITE_DEPLOY_TOKEN), clears website/static/bvar-scenario/, copies app/out/* in, and commits/pushes (a no-op git diff --cached --quiet short-circuits when nothing changed).

The Hugo build that assembles the final academic site is not in this workflow file — it runs as part of the website repo's downstream build that publishes to GitHub Pages, and any Hugo version pin lives in the starter-hugo-academic repo, not here (see below).

docs.title: BASE_PATH
docs.text:
The site is served from the /bvar-scenario sub-path of the academic site (GitHub Pages), so the static export must be built with that prefix. next.config.mjs:

const nextConfig = {
  output: 'export',
  basePath: process.env.BASE_PATH || '',
  assetPrefix: process.env.BASE_PATH || '',
  images: { unoptimized: true },
  env: { NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH: process.env.BASE_PATH || '' },
};
Build BASE_PATH Asset/link prefix Local dev / preview unset → '' root-relative (/…) Deploy (CI) /bvar-scenario /bvar-scenario/…

The CI step always sets BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario. Reproduce the exact deploy artifact locally with BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario npm run build. Client code that needs to fetch a shipped data file under the prefix reads process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH.

docs.title: Hugo version pin (0.122.0)
docs.text: The website repo's build is pinned to Hugo 0.122.0. The pin is enforced in the website pipeline (.github/workflows/updater-wip.yml). Rationale: Hugo 0.123+ removed the getCSV / getCSS data helpers that the wowchemy (academic) theme depends on; later versions break the site build. Do not bump the pin without first migrating the theme off those removed helpers. (Documented in README.md and ARCHITECTURE_FOR_AI.md.)

docs.title: What to commit (and what not to)
docs.text:
• Commit only deploy-relevant files (app/**, docs, config).
  • Leave pipeline/ scratch — diagnostics, staging dirs, raw CSVs, _verify/ — untracked.
  • Never commit app/.env.local or any FRED_API_KEY.

docs.title: Quick reference
docs.text:
# Full local green gate (run before every deploy):
cd app
npx tsc --noEmit
npm test                # fast suite (excludes the heavy solver canaries)
npm run test:heavy      # heavy solver / perf-parity canaries (1 fork)
BASE_PATH=/bvar-scenario npm run build

# Or sweep everything in one pass (slower, default parallelism):
npm run test:all        # = vitest run

# Run a single suite while iterating:
npx vitest run src/__tests__/compute/gvar-coupling-artifact.test.ts

# Regenerate the shipped coupling artifact (keeps the coupling canary green
# after a model refresh) — see REESTIMATING_MODELS.md:
npx vite-node scripts/precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs

If the gvar-coupling-artifact canary goes red after a model refresh, the artifact is stale: regenerate coupling_default.json.gz with the precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs script (full data-refresh runbook in REESTIMATING_MODELS.md).

Drag-off-the-heavy-path GVAR UX (optimistic pin + right-click-to-plant + Compute-gated full solve), a consistent re-estimation lifting the coupling spectral radius to ρ(M) = 0.924, the fix_fiscal_meta_indices.mjs re-index guard for the EA/UK debt explosion, the produced out-of-sample horse-race results, and a documented known-issues list.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• pipeline/fix_fiscal_meta_indices.mjs — fiscal-meta re-index (and a MANDATORY runbook RULE). fiscal_meta.json stores the fiscal-driver columns by position (idx = {gdp, deflator, revenue, primaryExp, effRate, sfa}); the 33-bloc GVAR-EM refit inserted variables ahead of the fiscal block in every spec except US, which silently re-pointed those positional indices at the wrong columns — EA's effRate landed on the equity index (~col 537) and the EA/UK debt-to-GDP path exploded. The new script re-resolves the indices by variable id from the deployed spec.json (order-independent) and rewrites all six fiscal metas, preserving d0/sfaDefault (it only touches meta.idx); it is idempotent. RULE (now documented in the maintainer runbooks): run node pipeline/fix_fiscal_meta_indices.mjs after ANY re-estimation/port that can change a fiscal bloc's variable order or count. The porter does not regenerate the meta, so this re-index is the fix — a green tsc/build with stale indices still ships a wrong debt path.
  • Optimistic conditioned-point pin (app/src/lib/conditionedDotY.ts). When you click, drag, or right-click to set a conditioned point, the marker (dot) now snaps to the clicked value instantly — it is reconstructed from the conditioning path (conditionedDotDisplayY), the exact inverse of the display→stored mapping (conditioningDeviationFromDisplayY), so it never waits for the (multi-second, on GVAR) forecast solve. The smoothed line + uncertainty bands reconcile around the pinned point once the solve lands. A single source of truth for the display↔stored round-trip prevents the two from drifting.
  • Right-click to plant a pin at the cursor. Right-clicking a forecast chart conditions that variable (if needed) and plants the conditioned point exactly at the cursor (the same forward mapping handleDrag uses, via onContextMenu in ChartGrid.tsx); left-clicking the "condition" button still toggles conditioning without moving a point.
  • Produced OOS empirical-evaluation results (separate repo em-bayesian-gvar-eval, results/tables.{txt,json} + results/tierA/us_rate_100bp.json). The recursive expanding-window horse-race — 56 origins (2010Q1–2024Q4, 2020 dropped) × 9 models, rolling as-of-t0 trade weights, CRPS-primary density score + 90% Model Confidence Set — now has numbers: AR(p)/DFM have the lowest point CRPS, but the EM-GVAR is in the inflation MCS at all horizons (h=1,2,4,8) and the short-rate MCS at h=1,2,4, statistically indistinguishable from the best on the nominal block, and decisively beats the textbook OLS classical GVAR (cgvar_a dropped from most MCS sets); emgvar_endo ⪰ emgvar_frozen in the MCS (best INFL h=1 relRMSE = emgvar_endo, 0.799). Honest caveats documented: the draw-based densities are over-confident (PIT cov90 ≈ 0.73–0.85 vs 0.90), the parametric log-score column is numerically broken on the under-covered clouds (CRPS + MCS are the robust primaries), and the Tier-A US-+100bp spillover is a multi-period conditional projection (large, sign-flipping magnitudes), not a causal IRF.
  • Known-issues / limitations sections in README.md and docs/MAINTAINER_OPERATIONS.md — five honestly-flagged, not-yet-fixed defects: (1) the CN fiscal debt inflation double-count (the snowball reads GDP_CN = nominal GDP plus a CPI deflator → inflation counted twice); (2) longRunTilt.ts does not filter non-finite long-run moments before the entropic-weight solve (a NaN draw can silently empty the anchor band); (3) the closed-form GVAR solve has no ρ(M)<1 divergence guard; (4) the conditional band uses the filtered (not DK-smoothed) covariance, overstating the band before a future pin; (5) the porter does not regenerate fiscal_meta (the stale-index cause of the EA/UK debt explosion — now guarded by the re-index RULE).

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Drag perf — the heavy GVAR solve is OFF the live drag path. On the live ~50-bloc US-endogenous panel every mean solve is multi-second (~4.5 s warm / ~13 s cold — the US-pinned structure misses the empty-pinStructure shipped coupling artifact), so the engine now withholds every solve during a drag (value-only or structure-change). The drag path is a cheap conditioning preview (the optimistic dot snaps to the cursor); exactly one cheap direct first-order solve fires on pointer-up; and the full cross-country fixed point + uncertainty bands run only on the "Solve full GVAR" button / top-bar Compute. A direct/full mode pill and a "computing…" badge make the mode explicit; the ≈95 MB coupling artifact is stripped from the worker post in direct mode. Drag state is a module-level signal bus (app/src/lib/compute/gvarDragState.ts) so begin/end/in-flight flags do not re-render the whole grid. Per-chart React.memo + reconciled chart references mean a single conditioning edit repaints one panel of ~35, not all of them.
  • Coupling spectral radius is now ρ(M) = 0.924 (was 0.896 / 0.896156 in an earlier refit). A later consistent re-estimation produced a new shipped coupling_default.json.gz (dim 2060, gzipped ≈ 31 MB) with a different modelSig and rhoSpectral = 0.924041 (still < 1, stationary; verified off the artifact header). Docs corrected to read the live value off the header rather than a copied number.
  • Docs accuracy pass. Corrected ρ(M) (0.896 → 0.924) across README.md, docs/ADDING_A_COUNTRY.md, docs/REESTIMATING_MODELS.md, docs/MAINTAINER_OPERATIONS.md (incl. the verify-command expect-line); fixed the fiscal bloc variable counts in README.md (EA 24, JP 23, UK 25, CA 25, CN 20 — previously conflated with the SFA estimation-sample lengths) and the stale GVAR-bloc count in USER_GUIDE.md (24 → 33, US now endogenous); replaced the stale ~0.45 s / ~3.4 s drag timings with the verified drag-off-the-heavy-path behaviour; documented the npm test (fast gate) vs npm run test:heavy split; and rewrote the generic app/README.md into a project-specific front-end guide.
Fiscal stationary prior + mean-reversion, GVAR coherence fixes, a full consistent 33-bloc refit, a per-economy variable table in the manual, and the out-of-sample empirical-evaluation harness.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Stationary white-noise (WN) prior for the fiscal GDP-ratios. FREV_GDP (revenue/GDP), FPEXP_GDP (primary expenditure/GDP) and SFA (stock-flow adjustment) are now estimated with a stationary white-noise prior — the own-first-lag prior mean is set to 0 (vs the default random-walk own-lag of 1) — so these mean-reverting shares revert to their in-sample means at the long end instead of drifting. Mechanism: the GLP prior in BVAR/MFBVAR/GLPreplicationWeb/bvarGLPmf.m zeroes the selected own-lag prior means (diagb(pos)=0; b(2:n+1,:)=diag(diagb)), threaded as the pos/stationary 3rd positional argument of cgl_bvarV5.m (function [...] = cgl_bvarV5(x,p,stationary,...), pos=stationary). The estimators pipeline/export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m (and the euro builder) read the per-variable prior from the spec (priorsfrom → statCols = find(Spec.Prior=="WN")) and pass it down. The effective debt rate EFFI stays RW (prior:RW, isFinancial:true). Result, e.g.: US revenue/GDP now mean-reverts to ~17.4% instead of being stuck near ~19% under the RW prior. The change is a no-op (byte-identical) for the 44 non-fiscal blocs (their statCols is empty). Verified in the deployed specs: US/EA/CA/UK/JP/CN FREV_GDP/FPEXP_GDP/SFA carry prior:WN, EFFI carries prior:RW. Committed in 6f0161a / 22250be / e38673a.
  • Out-of-sample empirical-evaluation harness (new Empirical Evaluation page; full design in the separate repo /home/farkas/github/em-bayesian-gvar-eval). A recursive expanding-window OOS forecast horse-race (proposed endogenous-US emgvar_endo + frozen-anchor emgvar_frozen vs six benchmarks: random walk + climatology, AR(p), single-country BVAR, textbook OLS GVAR, single-pass Bayesian GVAR, and a Bayesian DFM) plus a Tier-A endogenous-vs-frozen spillover contrast. The harness references the main tool via BVAR_TOOL_ROOT (it addpaths the estimator and imports gvarSolve.ts via vitest; it never copies estimator code). Origins 2010Q1–2024Q4, rolling as-of-t0 trade weights (the shipped 2021–2023 weights_full.json is forbidden in the sweep), four enforced look-ahead invariants, scoring by CRPS (primary) + parametric log-score + PIT/coverage, with Giacomini–White / Clark–West / Diebold–Mariano + Romano–Wolf + Model Confidence Set inference (matlab/oos_driver.m, eval/score.py, eval/tests.py, eval/tables.py). The empirical results are forthcoming/preliminary; the design is final.
  • Per-economy variable table in the methodology manual (docs/user_manual.tex, §Economy Coverage): every variable of every economy tabulated by transformation (log / lin) · prior (RW / WN) · financial tag, alongside new manual sections (the stationary-prior derivation, the EM-convergence and spectral-radius diagnostics, the empirical-results scaffold) and additional TikZ figures (architecture, Kalman, GVAR coupling, EM-trace schematic). Committed in fa104cd / 34d3e15.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• GLB global commons coherence — GLB_USEQ and GLB_OIL are now isFinancial:true everywhere. Both the US equity common (GLB_USEQ) and the oil common (GLB_OIL) are tagged financial across all blocs, so they are kept OBSERVED through the COVID-as-missing window (financials are not imputed) rather than NaN-masked with the macro block. Verified in the deployed EA spec (GLB_OIL/GLB_USEQ isFinancial:true, joining GLB_USLR/GLB_USBAA).
  • Canada industrial production (IP_CA) rebuilt as a log-LEVEL. The discontinued FRED level index is chained forward with the growth series to 2025Q4 and carried as a transformation:"log" log-level (pipeline/build_ca_ip_level.py), removing the level/growth incoherence in the CA bloc. Committed in 9839a46.
  • Full consistent GVAR-EM refit (33 blocs + coupling). All blocs were re-estimated together so the fiscal WN prior, the GLB financial tags, and the CA IP log-level are applied coherently across the whole system, with the coupling artifact regenerated. All 1048 per-economy variables were audited coherent (transformation · prior · financial). Committed in e38673a / 22250be.

Major release: the 24-bloc Global VAR with US-determined global commons (oil + US financial conditions), ~50-economy coverage, and a self-configuring cross-country solver. Breaking vs 1.x: the US anchor grew to n=37, every GVAR bloc carries 4 block:'global' commons, the whole panel is on a single 2025Q4 vintage (first forecast = 2026Q1), and the pre-shipped coupling artifact (coupling_default.json) is now mandatory for interactivity at 24 blocs.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Coverage expanded to ~50 economies. One registry (app/src/lib/data/countries.ts) now drives the dropdown: 6 core majors + 34 OECD members + 10 non-OECD emerging markets. Each is a single-country BVAR except the 24 that are GVAR-linked. Sources: FRED (cross-checked against national sources) for the core, Eurostat for EU/EFTA OECD, OECD-QNA / national-bank / IMF for the rest.
  • 24-bloc Global VAR (~90% of global demand). The GVAR was extended from 6 to 24 blocs: the US closed anchor + 23 Rest-of-World blocs (EA, JP, UK, CA, CN, CH, KR, MX, AU, TR, SE, PL, NO, DK, IN, BR, RU, ID, SA, AR, ZA, TH, MY). The bloc list + row-normalized trade weights live in app/public/data/bvar/gvar/weights_24bloc.json. The 23 RoW blocs are re-estimated to a cross-country fixed point by the GVAR-EM loop pipeline/export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m (each bloc's 3 stars built from partners' DK-completed series, iterated to convergence).
  • Global commons (shared US-determined variables). Each RoW bloc now also carries 4 block:'global' variables identical across blocs: GLB_OIL (WTI), GLB_USLR (US 10Y), GLB_USBAA (US BAA → credit spread), GLB_USEQ (S&P 500) — a standard GVAR global-variable treatment that transmits oil and US financial-conditions shocks to every bloc.
  • Self-configuring GVAR topology. configureGVARTopology/deriveBlocIdx (gvarSolve.ts) derive the active bloc set, per-bloc variable indices, and renormalized weights at load from the deployed spec.json files + weights_24bloc.json — no hardcoded IDX/W. A bloc is GVAR-active iff its spec carries block:'row' vars and it has a weights row.
  • Closed-form cross-country solve + precomputed coupling. The scenario fixed point is solved as x = (I − M)⁻¹d. At 24 blocs the in-browser inverse is infeasible (~19 min), so a precomputed coupling (M, (I − M)⁻¹, ρ) is shipped as app/public/data/bvar/gvar/coupling_default.json (regenerated by app/scripts/precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs) and hydrated into the solver cache, giving a sub-second matrix–vector solve — the key interactivity enabler for the full system.
  • User-defined long-run anchor (PLR). Pin a variable's long-run (≈10-year) forecast to a target; the posterior draws are entropically tilted toward it (per-draw weights, no re-estimation), pulling both the baseline and scenario fans (app/src/lib/compute/longRunTilt.ts). Single-country (non-GVAR) models only.
  • Global-common transmission in the solver (applyGlobalPins, gvarSolve.ts). The 4 block:'global' commons are now propagated end-to-end: deriveBlocIdx exposes them as IDX[c].glob (id → column), and each RoW bloc's global columns are pinned to its own baseline + the US scenario DEVIATION of the matching global (matched by id). A shock to US oil / US financial conditions therefore moves the identical common delta into every RoW bloc (verified on the real 24-bloc data: a US GLB_OIL pin gives an identical oil delta across all 23 RoW blocs to ≤1e-9), while each bloc's domestic variables respond through its own estimated coefficients. The forcing enters the per-bloc d, not the RoW↔RoW coupling M, so the shipped coupling artifact is unchanged. Gauss–Seidel ≡ closed-form to ≤1e-9 with globals present.

🔴 ASSUMPTION (load-bearing): the globals pin the US deviation on each bloc's own baseline, not the US level. Each RoW bloc estimated its own (differing) baseline path for the globals, so a level pin would inject a 17–70-unit spurious delta at zero scenario. The delta pin gives zero spillover at zero scenario and an identical common shock under conditioning. See docs/ARCHITECTURE_FOR_AI.md §5.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• US anchor re-estimated at n=37 (was n=36). The 4 global commons (GLB_OIL/GLB_USLR/GLB_USBAA/GLB_USEQ) are tagged block:'global' so the RoW blocs match them by id, and the DDEBT_GDP fiscal driver was replaced by EFFI ("Effective rate on debt, % p.a.", block:'fiscal') — the fiscal recursion now reads an estimated effective debt rate (fiscal_meta.idx.effRate) instead of the Δdebt-column shim.
  • All 50 blocs share a single 2025Q4 data vintage, so the first forecast ("nowcast") quarter is uniformly 2026Q1. app/src/lib/dates.ts now dates every bloc from the common 2025-10-01 panel end via lastHistOf() (the old per-bloc LAST_HIST_DATE map covered only 6 blocs at stale 2025Q2/Q3 vintages, leaving the other 44 with undated axes).

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• GVAR baseline ↔ engine consistency (mean AND bands regenerated). The 24 GVAR blocs' stored baseline was fully regenerated from the shipped companion so it reproduces what the app computes in-browser:
  • Means (app/scripts/regen_gvar_baselines.mjs): the deterministic all-NaN smoother on companion_mean.json, stored at full double precision. Previously they drifted 0.07–1.77 (100·log) vs the engine — a zero-scenario showed a small spurious deviation and the baseline-reproduction canary fired; now they reproduce to 0.
  • Bands q05/q16/q84/q95 (app/scripts/regen_gvar_bands.mjs): the predictive density the chart itself draws — bvarForecastDraw(paramsDraw, hist, H, addInnovation=true) over all 500 posterior draws (parameter draw + per-step innovation), with a seeded PRNG for byte-reproducibility, reduced to fractiles in both 100·log LEVEL space (baseline_levels.json) and DISPLAY space (baseline.json). Band widths match the prior export to ~MC noise; q05≤q16≤q84≤q95 and the mean sits inside the 90% band for every cell.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• COVID-as-missing re-estimation of all six models. The 2020 pandemic quarters are now treated as MISSING (NaN) and imputed with the Durbin–Koopman simulation smoother inside a data-augmentation Gibbs sampler (BVAR/MFBVAR/cgl_bvarV5.m): each sweep draws the missing data, updates the Minnesota hyperparameters (lambda, mu) by Metropolis–Hastings on the completed-data marginal likelihood, then draws the VAR coefficients. This is deliberately not Lenza–Primiceri (2022) COVID volatility scaling. The COVID window self-calibrates to the 2020Q2 GDP trough. New canonical estimators: pipeline/export_bvar_gvar_covid.m (EA/JP/UK/CA/CN), pipeline/export_bvar_us_covid.m (US, n=36, sur=0 so the 2-hyperparameter Gibbs is well-defined). The old export_bvar_gvar.m / export_bvar_us_fiscal.m (bvarGLP) are superseded.
  • lambda is now unconstrained / empirical Bayes for every model — the per-country lamfix (EA/JP/UK/CA 0.10, CN 0.04) is gone; with COVID imputed, the marginal-likelihood optimum is well-behaved (estimated lambda 0.24–0.56).
  • Uncertainty bands are now PREDICTIVE (parameter + innovation + missing-data forecast-error variance), from the Gibbs predictive density (Dforecast) in the export and bvarForecastDraw(..., addInnovation=true) in the browser. The old parameter-only bands (spread of posterior mean paths) collapsed under a tight prior and badly understated uncertainty. GDP 68% bands (q/q ann., pp): US 4.2, EA 5.0, JP 7.9, UK 9.5, CA 4.9, CN 13.5. The plotted mean stays the deterministic posterior-mean-coefficient forecast (so the unconditional smoother still reproduces it — gvar-convergence canary).

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • "Reset all" button (Scenario panel) — clears every scenario and all cross-country GVAR conditioning back to one empty default, behind an inline confirmation. Distinct from the per-scenario Reset (active scenario, current country only).

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• EA (and all GVAR blocs) density bands were too tight — the parameter-only methodology under EA's tight prior gave a ~1.9pp GDP band; fixed by the predictive bands + COVID-as-missing re-estimation above (~5pp).
  • No uncertainty band in the "% vs base" (deviation) view for GVAR blocs — the GVAR override was replacing the computed levelDeviationStd with the GVAR engine's null; now falls back to the single-country conditional std so the deviation band renders for EA et al.
  • Regenerated the US conforekf ground truth (pipeline/_verify/gt.m) for the scenario-scale-convention canary after the US re-estimation; companion draws compressed to 6 significant figures (US 52→26 MB).

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Full six-country GVAR. Every non-US model is now a GVAR bloc with its own three trade-weighted Rest-of-World stars (ROWDEM/ROWINF/ROWFIN): United States (closed anchor, n=36) + Euro Area (n=13), Japan (n=13), United Kingdom (n=15), Canada (n=15), and China (n=10). Conditioning any bloc propagates to all others through the shared RoW stars, solved as the linear cross-country fixed point Δ = M·Δ + d. The Gauss-Seidel and direct solvers agree to ~5e-11 across all six blocs (per-country and multi-country-mix, gvar-convergence.test.ts); the full system has spectral radius rho(M) ~ 0.59 and converges in ~10 sweeps. Trade weights row-normalized over the six blocs (pipeline/gvar_rawdata/weights_6bloc.json); the five non-US models are estimated by pipeline/export_bvar_gvar.m.
  • China as a full GVAR bloc. China is re-estimated as a 10-variable RoW bloc (7 domestic + 3 stars) at a tighter Minnesota prior lambda=0.04 — the wider 0.10 prior let the posterior mode extrapolate China's high-growth-2000s sample (~11% nominal GDP) far above the draw distribution, leaving export/import densities off-centre; the tighter prior re-centres the bands on the unconditional forecast. (Supersedes the earlier standalone single-country CN model; export_bvar_cn.m is deprecated.)
  • Reporting-units policy (displayUnits.ts). Index / FX / equity / commodity series (FXTWM, SP500, HP, PZTEXP, GSNE, NEER_EA, NEER_UK, NEER_CA, REER_CN, EQ_CN) are now displayed as the index level (e.g. 2010 = 100) instead of q/q-annualized growth — a trade-weighted FX or equity index q/q-ann. is not an informative number, the level is. Stored as 100·log(index) and conditioned in that space; only the display is exponentiated. baseline_levels.json now carries level-space quantiles (q16/q84/q05/q95/q50) for the level uncertainty fan, generated reproducibly inside the export scripts.
  • Level-space uncertainty fans for the level-displayed series, computed from the same posterior draws as the growth bands so the two are mutually consistent.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• All six countries refreshed to 2025Q4 from official national sources (Eurostat, ONS, StatCan, e-Stat, NBS) cross-checked against FRED, then re-estimated.
  • Unemployment-rate display bands floored at 0. LR, LR_JP, LR_UK, LR_CA can no longer render an impossible negative lower band. (A persistently-low central unemployment forecast remains a model question for re-estimation, not a display fix.)
  • GVAR cross-country slice now engages for all six blocs in the chart layer, not just US/EA/JP.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Experimental per-draw GVAR band dropped UK/CA/CN own-pins. A leftover 3-bloc ['US','EA','JP'] loop in ChartGrid.tsx (from the pilot) meant the experimental per-draw band for UK/CA/CN was computed without their own conditioning pins, so the band sat on the baseline while the line followed the propagated path. Now iterates the canonical six-bloc list.
  • Stale 3-bloc gvar/weights.json replaced with the six-bloc matrix (provenance only — the solver weights are compiled into gvarSolve.ts).
  • Pipeline header/dimension comments corrected to per-bloc sizes; export_bvar_cn.m marked DEPRECATED (running it would overwrite the live GVAR China model).

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Global VAR (GVAR) cross-country linkage. The Euro Area and Japan models now carry a Rest-of-World block of three trade-weighted "star" variables (foreign demand ROWDEM, foreign inflation ROWINF, foreign policy/financial conditions ROWFIN). Conditioning the US propagates to EA and JP, with EA<->JP back-feed, solved as a linear cross-country fixed point Δ = M·Δ + d at solve time. Two solvers (Gauss-Seidel and direct/doubling) agree to ~1e-11; the US->EA->JP pilot converges in ~4 sweeps (rho(M)~0.29). The solve runs in a debounced, stale-guarded Web Worker with a convergence read-out (sweeps, rho, ms). Trade weights come from the IMF IMTS database (build_trade_weights.py); the star series are built by build_gvar_data.py; EA/JP are re-estimated by export_bvar_gvar.m. New engine: gvarSolve.ts, runGvarSolve.ts, runGvarBandSolve.ts, gvarWorker.ts, useGvarForecast.ts, gvarLoader.ts.
  • US fiscal block (stock-flow-consistent). The US BVAR estimates government current-receipts and primary-expenditure ratios as drivers; primary/overall balance and the debt-to-GDP path are derived outside the VAR by the budget-identity recursion (CBO / IMF financial-programming style). Debt is parametrized as the change in debt/GDP and accumulated (debt = d0 + cumsum(Δd)). Conditioning a driver flows through to the derived debt path. Methodology: docs/fiscal-block-methodology.md; engine: fiscalAccounting.ts.
  • Per-draw GVAR uncertainty bands. In GVAR mode the uncertainty bands re-solve the cross-country system per posterior draw, so derived (RoW and fiscal) series get a band that is correctly centred on the propagated mean path rather than the baseline. Draw subsampling uses a deterministic stratified stride (reproducible, full tail coverage).
  • Auto-updating US dataset. build_us_auto.py rebuilds the US model hands-off from public FRED series, with the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) as the policy instrument (the 2-year yield moves to the financial block). The model auto-detects its sample span (currently to 2025Q4).
  • China (CN) added as the sixth country — a real estimated BVAR from public FRED data (17 variables, 3 lags: nominal GDP, industrial production, CPI, 3M rate, exports, imports, real effective FX, Shanghai equity + a US block), replacing an earlier synthetic placeholder. Built by pipeline/build_country_data.py + pipeline/export_bvar_cn.m.
  • Opt-in experimental features — a lower-left toggle. The first experimental feature is the per-draw iterative GVAR density (re-solves the cross-country system for each posterior draw); the default band is the faster model-specific density (see Changed).
  • Dated (calendar) x-axis on all charts, with date+value hover and dated Excel export. The left-hand conditioning sliders keep the NC / 1Q / 2Q / 3Q labels.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• US model re-estimated at n=36 with the fiscal drivers and FEDFUNDS policy rate. The Minnesota tightness is auto-selected; redundant real federal-consumption (GFH, a ~0.98-correlated subcomponent of government spending) is dropped, which restores a healthy auto-lambda (~0.28) and well-behaved conditioning.
  • EA/JP RoW models estimated with a fixed Minnesota tightness lambda=0.10: the auto-lambda optimum (~0.5-0.6) was too loose for the small n=15, T~90 systems and produced oscillating unconditional baselines; the fixed moderate-tight prior yields smooth, mean-reverting forecasts.
  • Fiscal reporting charts simplified. Under the change-in-debt/GDP parametrization the effective interest rate is backed out of the identity and degenerates to approximately nominal growth, so the effective-rate, interest (debt-service), and overall-deficit charts were removed; revenue, primary expenditure, primary balance, and debt/GDP are kept.
  • Default scenario uncertainty band reworked. The default band is now the model-specific (unconditional) forecast density parallel-shifted onto the propagated scenario path — it keeps the full forecast width (it no longer collapses when only a point or two is conditioned) and centres on the displayed line. The earlier per-draw iterative cross-country re-solve (which compressed the band and was slow) is preserved behind the experimental toggle.
  • Drag and band performance. Dragging now coalesces to one update per animation frame (rAF); posterior draws load as Float64Array (faster worker transfer + indexing); a US-viewed band skips the frozen-US's irrelevant EA/JP cross-country re-solve; and an unused Cholesky per draw was removed.

docs.title: Removed
docs.text: • Germany (DE) model removed from the platform.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Fiscal policy counterfactuals -- autonomous government spending shocks via DSGE fiscal_ IRFs. 60 models support fiscal shocks (including US_SW07, EA_SW03, EA_QUEST3). Fiscal and monetary adjustments can be combined, with separate Toeplitz convolutions for each instrument and summed DSGE-implied deltas.
  • Barnichon & Mesters optimal policy -- the "Compute optimal rate path" button now runs the B&M (2023) QP optimizer using the selected model's IRFs and lambda weights to generate an optimal rate adjustment path.
  • Policy rule parameters -- selecting a rule updates displayed parameters (read-only); parameters are embedded in the DSGE equilibrium and require Dynare to change.
  • Editable scenario names -- scenarios can be named in the control panel; names appear in chart titles and exported files.
  • Improved exports -- Excel exports with per-variable sheets (Q16/Median/Q84 + deviations), PNG as ZIP, PDF as combined multi-page document.
  • Example scenario -- bundled "Inflation Surge (10%)" example with SW07/SW rule counterfactual, downloadable from the control panel.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Multi-country BVAR conditional forecasting for 5 economies: United States (33 variables), Euro Area (22), United Kingdom (22), Japan (22), and Canada (22). Each country BVAR includes companion-form VAR coefficients, posterior mean baselines, and historical data pre-computed from FRED series.
  • Durbin-Koopman (DK) Kalman smoother for conditional forecasting, ported from MATLAB (conforekf.m / VARcf_DKcks.m / runKF_DK.m). State-space representation with companion-form transition matrices and Cholesky-based simulation draws.
  • 155 DSGE models from the Macroeconomic Model Data Base (MMB) with pre-computed impulse response functions across 9 policy rules (Taylor, GR, LWW, SW, CEE, OW08, OW13, Coenen, CMR). Models span 6 categories: Estimated US (69), Calibrated (50), Estimated Euro Area (20), Adaptive Learning (11), Estimated/Calibrated Multi-Country (10), and Estimated Other-Country (9). A total of 726 model-rule IRF files are included.
  • Three counterfactual modes for policy analysis:
  • Hold targets -- condition on the scenario paths for all user-specified variables while applying a rate adjustment via the DK smoother.
  • Let adjust -- condition only on the policy rate adjustment and allow all macro variables to adjust freely through the BVAR dynamics.
  • IRF-consistent -- use DSGE model IRFs to compute structurally consistent inflation and output gap responses to the policy shock sequence (Toeplitz convolution), then condition the BVAR on these DSGE-implied deltas.
  • Barnichon and Mesters (2023, AER) optimal policy solver. Quadratic programming approach using sufficient statistics (DSGE IRFs) to find the optimal monetary policy shock sequence minimizing a weighted loss function over inflation and output gap deviations.
  • Per-variable, per-quarter conditioning via the control panel. Users can select any BVAR variable for conditioning, set deviation paths with sliders (-3 to +3 percentage points), and toggle individual quarters on or off. Conditioning paths are expressed relative to the DK smoother's unconditional forecast.
  • Interactive draggable chart interface using Plotly.js. Inflation, GDP growth, and policy rate charts support direct drag interaction on forecast points. Other variables can be conditioned via a "condition" button on each chart panel.
  • Uncertainty bands with two modes: Quick (analytical 16th/84th percentile bands from the DK smoother covariance) and Full (Monte Carlo simulation draws from the posterior distribution of companion matrices, loaded lazily from companion_draws.json).
  • Up to 4 simultaneous scenarios with color-coded overlays (blue, red, green, purple). Each scenario maintains independent model selection, policy rule, conditioning paths, counterfactual settings, and rate adjustments.
  • Excel export and import of scenario configurations. Export produces multi-sheet .xlsx files containing settings, conditioning paths, active quarters, rate adjustments, and a variable index map. Import supports both .xlsx and legacy .json formats, with robust variable matching across spec changes.
  • Full session export from the top bar, producing a single Excel workbook with one sheet per scenario including all settings, conditioning paths, and rate adjustments.
  • Levels vs. deviations display mode toggle, and quarter-over-quarter vs. year-over-year growth rate display for applicable variables.
  • Custom matrix algebra library (matrix.ts) using flat Float64Array storage in row-major order for browser-side linear algebra, including Cholesky decomposition, matrix solve, and Toeplitz construction.
  • Docker Compose deployment configuration with a Next.js frontend container (port 3000) and a Python backend container (port 8000) with FRED API key support and IRF caching volumes.

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• 14 DSGE models have no pre-computed IRFs (shown as having zero available rules in the catalog). These are primarily adaptive learning variants and two other models:
  • CA_ToTEM10 -- Murchison & Rennison (2006)
  • EAUS_NAWMctww -- Cogan et al. (2013)
  • NK_BGG99AL -- Bernanke et al. (1999) AL
  • NK_CDK24 -- Chan, Diz, & Kanngiesser (2024)
  • NK_CGG02AL -- Clarida et al. (2002) AL
  • NK_CGG99AL -- Clarida et al. (1999) AL
  • NK_IR04AL -- Ireland (2004) AL
  • NK_LWW03AL -- Levin et al. (2003) AL
  • NK_RW06AL -- Ravenna & Walsh (2006) AL
  • NK_RW97AL -- Rotemberg & Woodford (1997) AL
  • US_FM95AL -- Fuhrer & Moore (1995) AL
  • US_MI07AL -- Milani (2007)
  • US_SW07AL -- Slobodyan & Wouters (2012)
  • US_YR13AL -- Rychalovska (2016)
  • Docker backend not tested end-to-end. The docker-compose.yml defines frontend and backend services, but the backend (Python/MATLAB bridge for live BVAR estimation and MMB IRF computation) has not been validated in an integrated deployment.
  • China BVAR has limited data. The CN BVAR includes 23 variables with baseline and companion matrices, but lacks companion_draws.json (no full uncertainty bands), baseline_levels.json, and historical_levels.json. The underlying data does not come from FRED and may have coverage gaps.
  • IRF-consistent mode falls back silently to let-adjust when the selected DSGE model lacks IRFs for the chosen policy rule. A small warning is shown in the UI but no explicit user confirmation is required.

docs.title: Technical Notes
docs.text:
• All BVAR computation (Kalman filtering, DK smoothing, conditional forecasting, uncertainty simulation) runs entirely client-side in the browser using TypeScript and Float64Array-backed matrix operations. No server round-trips are required after initial data loading.
  • Pre-computed data totals approximately 152 MB, served as static JSON from /public/data/. This includes BVAR posterior estimates for 6 countries, 726 DSGE model-rule IRF files, and the model/rule catalogs. The US companion_draws.json (posterior simulation draws for full uncertainty) is loaded lazily only when the user clicks Compute.
  • The unconditional DK smoother result is cached and reused across conditioning changes to avoid redundant computation. A 30ms debounce is applied to conditioning path updates.
  • Built with Next.js 14.2, React 18, Zustand 4.5 for state management, Plotly.js for interactive charts, and SheetJS (xlsx) for Excel I/O. Styled with Tailwind CSS. Tested with Vitest.

docs.title: Data Contracts & JSON Schemas
docs.text:
This page is the reference for every JSON contract the app reads from app/public/data/bvar/. It is the authority for what each file contains, the exact field names, the array shapes, and the conventions (variable ids, block tags, transformations, the 6-significant-figure compaction). It complements — and does not duplicate — the prose in the companion docs:

  • ARCHITECTURE_FOR_AI.md — how the compute engine uses these files (§4–§10).
  • README.md / USER_GUIDE.md — overview & UI.
  • fiscal-block-methodology.md — the math behind fiscal_meta.json.
  • ADDING_A_COUNTRY.md / REESTIMATING_MODELS.md — how the files are produced.

If a fact here disagrees with the code, the code wins — these contracts are verified against the live files (app/public/data/bvar/US, …/EA, …/DE, …/CN, …/gvar) but the data is re-ported on every re-estimation.

docs.title: 1. Where the files live
docs.text:
Each economy is one directory app/public/data/bvar/<CC>/ keyed by ISO-2-ish country code (US, EA, JP, …; EA = euro-area aggregate). The cross-country GVAR coupling lives in app/public/data/bvar/gvar/.

app/public/data/bvar/
  US/   EA/   JP/  …                 one directory per economy (~50)
    spec.json                        variable manifest (ids, blocks, transforms)
    companion_mean.json              posterior-mean VAR (betahat, sigmahat)
    companion_draws.json             flat posterior draws (lazy-loaded, large)
    baseline.json                    baseline fan in DISPLAY/GROWTH space
    baseline_levels.json             baseline fan in LEVEL space
    historical.json                  last ~40q in DISPLAY/GROWTH space
    historical_levels.json           last ~40q in LEVEL space
    transformations.json             per-variable 'log'|'lin' list
    fiscal_meta.json                 ONLY the 6 fiscal blocs (US/EA/JP/UK/CA/CN)
  gvar/
    weights_24bloc.json              33-bloc trade-weight matrix + topology source
    coupling_default.json.gz         gzipped precomputed (I−M)⁻¹ coupling artifact

Loader (app/src/lib/data/loader.ts). loadBVARData(country) fetches spec/companion_mean/baseline/historical eagerly, then optionally baseline_levels/historical_levels/fiscal_meta (a 404 → null, which is normal for standalone economies that ship no fiscal_meta.json). companion_draws.json is loaded lazily by loadCompanionDraws(country) only when Compute is clicked (it is the largest file — tens of MB) and its flat arrays are converted once to Float64Array for fast indexing and zero-copy structured-clone into the band worker.

docs.title: 2.1 The data space (log / lin)
docs.text:
Every variable has a transformation:

transformation stored as chart display "log" 100·log(level) q/q annualised growth = 4·Δ(100·log level) "lin" the level itself (rates, ratios, %-of-GDP, sentiment indices) the level as-is
  • *.json files in display/growth space (baseline.json, historical.json) carry the already-differenced 4·Δ(100·log level) for log vars and the level for lin vars.
  • *_levels.json files carry 100·log(level) for log vars and the level for lin vars (this is the "level space" that feeds the fiscal recursion, the GVAR solver, and the level-display fans).

This is the single most bug-prone convention in the codebase — a stray ×4 or /100 between the two spaces diverges forecasts silently. The canary test scenario-scale-convention.test.ts pins it.

docs.title: 2.2 The 6-significant-figure compaction
docs.text:
The eight ported per-bloc files are rounded to 6 significant figures and serialised as compact JSON (separators=(",",":"), no whitespace) by pipeline/port_gvar_exports.py (sig6 → float(f"{x:.6g}"), recursing through lists/dicts via compact). The compacted set is exactly the FILES list in that script: spec.json, companion_mean.json, companion_draws.json, historical.json, historical_levels.json, baseline.json, baseline_levels.json, transformations.json. Integers, None, strings, booleans, and ±inf/NaN pass through (0/±inf/NaN map to 0/themselves). So in those files expect values like 8.04724, 1009.29, -1.45722 — never full double precision.

coupling_default.json.gz is also 6-sigfig: its builder (app/scripts/precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs) rounds M/invImM/rhoSpectral with round6 = x => Number(x.toPrecision(6)) before gzip.

Two files are NOT 6-sigfig — they are written full precision by their own builders and are not in port_gvar_exports.py's FILES:

  • fiscal_meta.json — emitted by pipeline/build_fiscal_<cc>_4var.py with json.dump(meta, indent=2); d0 is full double precision (e.g. 87.84509550831034), sfaDefault is round(sfa_mean, 4) (4 decimals).
  • weights_24bloc.json — only the US receiver row is 6-sigfig; the other 32 rows carry full double precision (e.g. 0.15734894312225214).

Do not assume 6-sigfig when validating these two files.

docs.title: 2.3 Variable ids, blocks, isFinancial
docs.text:
Variable ids are stable string keys (e.g. GDPH, POLRATE, HICP_EA, GLB_OIL, ROWDEM_US). Suffixes encode role:

  • GLB_* — a global common (the 4 US-determined commons, ids GLB_OIL/GLB_USLR/GLB_USBAA/GLB_USEQ). These ids are shared across the GVAR blocs (US, EA, JP, …). The euro FSAP standalones carry the same four series in their block:'global' columns but under their US-exogenous ids WTI_US/FCM10_US/FBAA_US/EQ_US (not GLB_*) — resolve by block, not by id.
  • ROW{DEM,INF,FIN}_<CC> — a bloc's 3 trade-weighted stars.
  • _<CC> on a domestic var (GDP_EA, RATE_DE) — the country's own series.
  • _US on an exogenous regressor in a euro FSAP standalone (GDPH_US, FCM10_US).

Each variable also carries a block tag that drives the GVAR/fiscal topology (read at runtime by configureGVARTopology / deriveBlocIdx in gvarSolve.ts — never hardcode indices):

block meaning where it appears domestic the bloc's own endogenous macro/financial vars every model fiscal the 4 fiscal drivers {FREV_GDP, FPEXP_GDP, EFFI, SFA} the 6 fiscal blocs (US/EA/JP/UK/CA/CN) row the 3 trade-weighted stars ROW{DEM,INF,FIN}_<CC> GVAR-coupled blocs (incl. endogenous US) global the 4 US-determined commons (oil, US 10y, US Baa, US equity) GVAR blocs (as GLB_*) and euro FSAP standalones (as WTI_US/FCM10_US/FBAA_US/EQ_US) external EA-shared exogenous regressors (EA policy rate, EA corp spread, member rate) euro FSAP standalones only us US-linked exogenous regressors (GDPH_US, JGDP_US, FCM2_US, …) euro FSAP standalones only

A bloc is GVAR-active iff its spec.json carries block:'row' variables and it has a row in weights_24bloc.json. The US is endogenous iff its spec carries the 3 ROW*_US stars and the weights file has a US row — this sets the module flag US_ENDOGENOUS. See ARCHITECTURE_FOR_AI.md §5/§5a.

isFinancial (boolean) flags financial variables (rates, spreads, FX, equity, house prices, leverage, oil). It drives display units and the COVID treatment (financials are kept observed through 2020Q2–Q4 while macro vars are masked).

docs.title: 3. spec.json — the variable manifest
docs.text:
The authoritative list of variables in fixed column order. The engine reads n and the per-variable metadata; it is index-agnostic otherwise (resolves fiscal/GVAR columns by id, not position).

Field Type Shape Meaning n int scalar number of endogenous variables (= length of variables) variables array [n] one entry per variable, in column order variables[i].id string stable variable key (column identity) variables[i].name string human-readable label (for the UI dropdown) variables[i].transformation "log"|"lin" data-space (§2.1) variables[i].isFinancial bool financial-var flag (§2.3) variables[i].prior "RW"|"WN" prior-mean family on the own first lag (§3.1): "RW" = random walk (own-lag prior mean 1, the default); "WN" = stationary white-noise (own-lag prior mean 0 → mean-reverting). Only the three fiscal GDP-ratios (FREV_GDP,FPEXP_GDP,SFA) carry "WN"; everything else is "RW" variables[i].block string topology tag (§2.3)

Example (US, n=41, abridged — first/fiscal/global/star columns):

{
  "n": 41,
  "variables": [
    {"id":"GDPH","name":"Real Gross Domestic Product","transformation":"log","isFinancial":false,"prior":"RW","block":"domestic"},
    {"id":"LR","name":"Civilian Unemployment Rate: 16 yr +","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":false,"prior":"RW","block":"domestic"},
    {"id":"POLRATE","name":"Effective Federal Funds Rate (policy rate)","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"domestic"},
    {"id":"GLB_USLR","name":"10-Year Treasury Note Yield","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"global"},
    {"id":"FREV_GDP","name":"Federal Receipts (% of GDP)","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":false,"prior":"WN","block":"fiscal"},
    {"id":"FPEXP_GDP","name":"Federal Primary Expenditure (% of GDP)","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":false,"prior":"WN","block":"fiscal"},
    {"id":"EFFI","name":"Federal Effective Interest Rate (% p.a.)","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"fiscal"},
    {"id":"SFA","name":"Stock-Flow Adjustment (% of GDP)","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":false,"prior":"WN","block":"fiscal"},
    {"id":"GLB_OIL","name":"WTI crude oil ($/bbl)","transformation":"log","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"global"},
    {"id":"ROWDEM_US","name":"US RoW Demand","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"row"},
    {"id":"ROWINF_US","name":"US RoW Inflation","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"row"},
    {"id":"ROWFIN_US","name":"US RoW Fin","transformation":"lin","isFinancial":true,"prior":"RW","block":"row"}
  ]
}

The US carries the 4 globals as its own endogenous vars (cols where block:'global') plus its 3 ROW*_US stars (cols 38–40, block:'row') — this is what makes the US endogenous. A RoW bloc (EA, n=24) instead carries the globals as weakly-exogenous commons that the solver pins to the US-determined path. A euro FSAP standalone (DE, n=23) carries no row block — its foreign side is external/us/global exogenous regressors.

docs.title: 3.1 The prior field — "RW" (default) vs "WN" (stationary fiscal ratios)
docs.text:
variables[i].prior selects the prior mean on each variable's own first lag in the GLP/Minnesota prior. It takes exactly two values:

prior own-first-lag prior mean implied behaviour who carries it "RW" 1 (random walk — the historical default) the series can drift at the long end (a unit-root prior centre) every variable except the three fiscal GDP-ratios "WN" 0 (white noise — stationary) the series mean-reverts to its in-sample mean only FREV_GDP, FPEXP_GDP, SFA (in all 6 fiscal blocs)

Why this exists. Under a pure "RW" prior the fiscal GDP-ratios (revenue, primary expenditure, stock-flow adjustment) inherited a unit-root centre and drifted over the 20-quarter projection — e.g. US federal receipts stuck at ~19% of GDP instead of reverting to their ~17.4% in-sample mean. Switching them to "WN" centres the own-lag prior at 0 so the ratios mean-revert to their sample means at the long horizon, which is the economically correct stationary behaviour for a bounded share-of-GDP. EFFI (the effective interest rate) stays "RW" — it is a slow-moving level, not a bounded ratio. This change is committed (6f0161a/22250be/e38673a) and is a no-op (byte-identical export) for the 44 non-fiscal blocs, which carry no "WN" variable.

The mechanism (verified end-to-end in the estimator).

  1. Spec → pos. The MATLAB estimators read the per-variable prior string out of spec.json and build the index vector of stationary columns:
  2. pipeline/export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m (statCols=find(Spec.Prior(keep)=="WN")', on the kept domestic block; stars are appended after and are always "RW").
  3. pipeline/export_bvar_euro_fsap.m (statCols = find(Prior=="WN")'). Both resolve via a robust priorsfrom(...) helper (handles MATLAB's jsondecode dropping a prior field that is present on only some variables; absent → all-"RW").
  4. pos → cgl_bvarV5. statCols is threaded as the 3rd positional argument (stationary) of BVAR/MFBVAR/cgl_bvarV5.m, where pos=stationary (line 19) and passed on as 'pos',pos to bvarGLPmf.
