docs.text: Bloc Variables Genuine end Tail CN FREV_GDP, FPEXP_GDP, EFFI, SFA 2024Q4 ~4q; gross_debt linearly extrapolated (+3862.5/q) in CN fiscal raw JP FREV_GDP 37.1068, FPEXP_GDP 37.0881, EFFI 0.804958 2025Q2 2q flat in the 2025Q4 panel; SFA contaminated US ROWDEM_US, ROWINF_US, ROWFIN_US 2025Q4 1q held star (verified); SFA recomputed (§2) SA LR_SA 2025Q1 4q held ZA GOV_ZA 2025Q1 4q held TW RATE_TW 2024Q1 7q flat (may be genuine CBC hold — needs-human-check)

docs.title: 1E. OFF-BY-ONE date mislabel (value is REAL 2025Q4, plotted as 2026Q1)
docs.text:
dates.ts DEFAULT_LAST_HIST_DATE = 2026-01-01 (commit ae34cd9, US pilot) advanced the axis-end label without a re-pull for non-US blocs; refresh-quarter.mjs has a source registry only for US and is fail-closed for every other bloc. So the shipped last row is a genuine 2025Q4 observation mislabeled one quarter forward — no value fabricated, the date is wrong. Verified across L..N (Appendix B) and DE/FR (§0): shipped idx39 == genuine 2025Q4, idx38 == genuine 2025Q3, including the global commons columns.

Affected (last row = genuine 2025Q4, value real): DE, DK, ES, FI, FR, GR (flag-only; genuine 2026Q1 exists at source, unpulled); LT, LU, LV, MX, MY, NL, NO, NZ (needs-human-check, re-date); PL, PT (VERIFIED +1Q shift); SA, SE, SI, SK, TR, TW, UK, ZA (build-2025Q4 relabel). For slow series (HICP/HPI/fiscal/BIS-credit) the source genuinely ends 2025Q4 (no 2026Q1 exists) — the honest edge is 2025Q4 regardless. MY raw panel actually reaches 2026Q1 but the shipped row is the stale 2025Q4.

docs.title: Genuine — keep-all-real (no action)
docs.text: US (41 cols, except held stars/SFA §2); IL (stale 2024Q4, genuine); IS/HU/IT (genuine 2025Q4, label-stale only); AT/BE/CZ/CH Eurostat macro+HICP+fiscal; AU/CA IMF-mirror GDP; global commons where the bloc window reaches real FRED 2026Q1; BIS-credit and None ragged tails (missing); recomputed GVAR stars / SFA / EFFI identities where their genuine inputs reach the panel end.

docs.title: 2. THE 2026Q1 US REFRESH VERDICT
docs.text:
US is the ONLY genuinely-refreshed bloc (the refresh-quarter.mjs SOURCES registry exists only for US, n≈41, replay-and-diff gated). Verified live and against shipped:

  • Real FRED 2026Q1 pulls — nothing made up: GDPC1, PCECC96, GDPDEF, PCEPI, CPIAUCSL, UNRATE (4.3 == shipped), FEDFUNDS (3.64 == shipped), DGS2/5/10, BAA, AAA, SP500, WTISPLC, FGRECPT/GDP (FREV moved 18.77→18.45), fiscal expenditure, EFFI (identity, moved 3.26→3.16). All keep-all-real.

  • Two NON-genuine US 2026Q1 tail items — both directive-flagged, both I verified:

  • ROWDEM_US / ROWINF_US / ROWFIN_US are HELD-LAST — 2026Q1 == 2025Q4 (last two values identical, verified). A single-bloc US refresh can't recompute trade-weighted partner aggregates; a full cross-bloc star refresh is needed for a genuine 2026Q1. This is an extrapolation.
  • SFA is a RECOMPUTED one-step debt-recursion RESIDUAL, not a pulled series (fiscal_sfa). 2026Q1 SFA (1.23) is the app's identity output; the observed debt/GDP d0 it needs for 2026Q1 may itself be provisional.

Verdict: the US directly-sourced macro/financial/fiscal columns for 2026Q1 are genuine FRED data — not fabricated. The only non-genuine US 2026Q1 cells are the 3 held-last stars (extrapolation) and the recomputed SFA residual. Truncate those 4 to 2025Q4 (or regenerate the stars via a cross-bloc refresh) before a clean publish.

docs.title: 3. DROP PLAN — per-bloc honest ragged edge
docs.text:
Three actions: truncate-tail-to-genuine (keep real history, drop synthetic tail), drop-variable (proxy/fabricated end-to-end), re-date (value real, label wrong → shift label, no data loss).

Bloc Action New genuine last obs Notes CN drop-variable GDP_CN; truncate/re-pull RATE/REER/EQ/CPI; truncate fiscal RATE/REER/EQ re-pullable→2026Q1; CPI→2025Q1; fiscal→2024Q4; GDP_CN gone or re-source real GDP principal offender; nominal-GDP proxy taints the CN block KR truncate-tail 2024Q1 drop 7 synthetic q; OR re-source (live FRED NGDPRSAXDCKRQ exists) RU truncate-tail 2024Q1 (POLRATE_RU/HP_RU genuine to 2025Q4) drop 8 synthetic q for GDP/CPI/EXP/LR/RATE/NEER TH truncate-tail 2025Q2 (POLRATE/HP/CREDIT 2025Q4) drop 3 WEO + 1 relabel; NESDC 2026Q1 exists ID truncate-tail 2025Q1 drop 3 WEO q IN truncate-tail 2025Q2 drop 2 backfill q CR flag/drop GDP_CR (IP proxy) macro 2025Q4 (re-date) decide IP-as-GDP acceptability CL/CN/IN flag CORP_* (sovereign→corp) n/a keep w/ disclosure OR drop the proxy column US truncate stars+SFA to 2025Q4 (or regen stars) 2026Q1 (sourced); stars/SFA→2025Q4 only 4 cols affected DE,DK,ES,FI,FR,GR re-date to 2025Q4 (or re-pull genuine 2026Q1) 2025Q4 values real; HICP/HPI/fiscal/credit genuinely end 2025Q4 PL,PT re-date / truncate (+1Q shift VERIFIED) 2025Q4 values real 2025Q4 mislabeled LT,LU,LV,MX,MY,NL,NO,NZ re-date (set LAST_HIST_DATE override 2025-10-01) 2025Q4 LENDR_MX truncate (genuine null tail) SA,SE,SI,SK,TR,TW,UK,ZA re-date / truncate relabel 2025Q4 AE blocs (UK/SE/SI/SK) can re-pull genuine 2026Q1 AR,AU,BR,CA,CO needs-human-check (EM-swarm/IMF-SDMX not FRED-re-pullable) mostly 2025Q4 §5 IL none 2024Q4 genuine, just stale IS,HU,IT re-date label only 2025Q4 genuine AT,BE,CH,CZ none / minor re-date 2025Q4–2026Q1 live Eurostat, mostly genuine EA,EE,IE AUDIT — not assessed unknown §6

docs.title: 4. ESTIMATION-PANEL IMPLICATION (the decision that matters)
docs.text:
The synthetic data WAS in the 2025Q4 estimation panel. The model saw it.

build_gvar_2025q4.py (verified header) WEO-extends short domestic series quarter-by-quarter "so the GVAR re-estimation has a balanced panel" and truncates anything past 2025Q4. So the KR (7q), RU (8q), TH (4q), ID (3q), IN (2q) fabricated tails, the CN nominal-GDP-proxy backfill, and every held-last column entered the estimated companion (companion_mean.json, companion_draws.json). The posterior VAR coefficients, GVAR coupling, and predictive bands were fit on a panel containing synthetic observations — for KR/RU at the final 7–8 quarters, the most forecast-relevant edge.

refresh-quarter.mjs keeps the companion FROZEN; the 2026Q1 refresh only re-anchors the display (re-projects the fixed companion on refreshed history), no re-estimation.

Therefore display-truncation does NOT suffice for the deep-synthesis blocs. Truncating the chart edge hides the synthetic points but leaves a model estimated on them. Two tiers:

  • Tier 1 (re-estimate OR re-source) — synthetic baked into the estimated companion: CN, KR, RU, TH, ID, IN. A genuinely "no synthetic data" publish implies a real-only re-estimation on a ragged panel (or re-source of genuine 2026Q1 where it exists — KR/TH/RU GDP have live alternatives).
  • Tier 2 (re-date / truncate display only) — values genuine, dating/refresh artifact: all off-by-one & +1Q-relabel blocs, plus the US held-stars/SFA.

docs.title: 5. UNVERIFIABLE / needs-human-check (DO NOT assume real)
docs.text:
Not re-pulled live this run; human-verify before keeping:

  • AR/AU/BR/CA/CO/CR macro (EM-swarm / IMF-SDMX / BIS / national; not FRED-re-pullable): GDP/CONS/INV/GOV/EXP/IMP/CPI/LR/RATE/NEER/LENDR. Short identical-trailing runs to flag as possibly-held: AR GOV (4q), AR POLRATE @29 (4q), BR GOV (4q).
  • AU/CA national-accounts components (CONS/INV/GOV/EXP/IMP): OECD-MEI FRED IDs froze 2023Q3; live national source un-pulled. (GDP itself uses the live IMF-mirror — genuine 2026Q1.)
  • BIS NFC-credit CREDIT_GDP_* for ID/IN/IT/JP/KR: BIS publishes ~1Q lag; moving tails likely a separate later pull but the vintage end is unconfirmed.
  • LENDR_ / CORP_ (IMF IFS / ECB MIR / national): not individually re-pulled.
  • SI/SK CREDIT_GDP (unverifiable): FRED QSI/QSKNAM770A return 400 (BIS not on FRED).
  • TW CREDIT_GDP (constructed via build_credit_hardgap_tw); TW RATE_TW 7q flat (possible genuine CBC hold — verify); UK IP_UK & CPI_UK (FRED MEI frozen 2024Q1/2025Q1 — verify build source vs ONS).
  • JP SFA (residual contaminated by held FREV/FPEXP/EFFI beyond 2025Q2).
  • IT FREV/FPEXP (possible temporal-disaggregation of annual gov-finance).
  • RU LENDR (IMF/CBR splice end unconfirmed).
  • AT/BE/CZ LR (Eurostat dimension mismatch on re-pull — bloc live-sourced, column unconfirmed).

docs.title: 6. GAPS in this audit
docs.text:
• EA, EE, IE on disk but NOT in the supplied audit — unassessed. Audit before any clean publish.
  • This run verified the category exemplars (CN, US, KR, RU, DE) against shipped bytes; §5 items were taken from the audit at face value and explicitly NOT assumed genuine.

docs.title: EXEC SUMMARY (for the user — you decide the drop approach)
docs.text:
Headline counts - 6 blocs carry deep WEO-fabricated tails BAKED INTO the estimated companion (the model saw them): KR (7q, genuine ends 2024Q1 — WORST), RU (8q, 2024Q1), TH (4q), ID (3q), IN (2q), CN. - ~24 blocs are an OFF-BY-ONE / +1Q RELABEL — value real 2025Q4, dated 2026Q1; pure dating fix, no fabricated numbers: DE, DK, ES, FI, FR, GR, LT, LU, LV, MX, MY, NL, NO, NZ, PL, PT, SA, SE, SI, SK, TR, TW, UK, ZA. - ~6 proxy variables (wrong concept): CN GDP_CN (nominal→real, worst, also 9q backfilled), CR GDP_CR (IP→real GDP), CN/CL/IN CORP_* (sovereign→corporate yield). - 3 CN frozen-mirror flat tails (RATE/REER/EQ, 3q) where genuine FRED data EXISTS and is just un-pulled — cheapest fix.

Worst offenders: KR (genuine ends 2024Q1; 7 synthetic quarters in the estimation panel) and RU (2024Q1; 8 synthetic quarters); CN GDP_CN (a nominal-GDP series labelled real — spec name confirms — 9q WEO-backfilled; taints the CN block; drop or re-source).

US-refresh verdict: US is the only genuinely-refreshed bloc; its directly-sourced 2026Q1 macro/financial/fiscal columns are real FRED pulls — nothing fabricated (UNRATE 4.3, FEDFUNDS 3.64, GDPC1, fiscal — verified live==shipped). The only non-genuine US 2026Q1 cells are the 3 held-last foreign stars (ROWDEM/ROWINF/ROWFIN, 2026Q1==2025Q4, verified) and the recomputed SFA residual — both directive-flagged, both confirmed. Truncate those 4 to 2025Q4 (or regen stars).

Recommended drops: 1. drop-variable GDP_CN (nominal-proxy + 9q backfill) — or re-source genuine CN real GDP. 2. truncate-to-genuine the deep-WEO blocs: KR→2024Q1, RU→2024Q1, TH→2025Q2, ID→2025Q1, IN→2025Q2, CN-fiscal→2024Q4, CN frozen-mirror→re-pull. 3. truncate US stars + SFA to 2025Q4. 4. re-date the ~24 off-by-one blocs to 2025Q4 (or re-pull genuine 2026Q1 for AE/Eurostat blocs that publish it) — no data loss.

Estimation caveat (load-bearing): the synthetic tails for the 6 Tier-1 blocs were in the estimation panel (build_gvar_2025q4.py WEO-balances before the GVAR re-estimation; refresh-quarter.mjs keeps the companion FROZEN). Display-truncation alone is NOT enough for CN/KR/RU/TH/ID/IN — the estimated companion was fit on fabricated data, so a clean publish implies a real-only re-estimation (or re-source) for those blocs. The off-by-one / +1Q-relabel blocs and the US stars/SFA are display/refresh artifacts on genuine values → re-date / truncate suffices.

Not assumed real (verify first): AR/AU/BR/CA/CO/CR macro (EM-swarm/IMF-SDMX), BIS-credit vintages (ID/IN/IT/JP/KR), all LENDR_/CORP_, SI/SK/TW constructed credit, TW RATE flat, UK IP/CPI MEI-frozen, JP/IT fiscal. EA/EE/IE were NOT audited.

docs.title: Appendix A — detailed sub-audit, blocs [A..C] (AR, AT, AU, BE, BR, CA, CH, CL, CN, CO, CR, CZ)
docs.text: Branch improve-live @ 82cc6ab. READ-ONLY. COVID mask (2020Q2–2021Q1 NaN) and None/NaN ragged tails are honest MISSING data, NOT synthetic. Method: re-pulled each series' LIVE source (FRED key in pipeline/build_country_data.py, Eurostat SDMX 2.1, BIS-via-FRED) and compared the genuine last quarter to the shipped historical_levels.json end per app/src/lib/dates.ts (2026Q1 default; CN held 2025Q4). Sources not re-pullable with the FRED key (DBnomics OECD-QNA, IMF-SDMX, national banks used by the EM swarm; BIS direct) → unverifiable / needs-human-check (NOT assumed real).

docs.title: Verified genuine (KEEP)
docs.text:
• Global commons in EVERY bloc: GLB_OIL=WTISPLC, GLB_USLR=DGS10, GLB_USBAA=BAA, GLB_USEQ=SP500 — all live to 2026Q1.
  • BIS credit %GDP Q<ISO2>NAM770A — live to 2025Q4 (AR/AU/BE/BR/CA/CL/CN/CO/CZ). 2026Q1 cell is honest-missing (None) for 2026Q1-ending blocs.
  • EU/EFTA (AT, BE, CZ, CH) built LIVE from Eurostat (not the frozen FRED OECD-MEI mirror): real GDP namq_10_gdp B1GQ → 2026Q1; HICP prc_hicp_midx → 2025Q4; AT/BE fiscal gov_10q_ggnfa/ggdebt → 2025Q4.
  • AU real GDP NGDPRSAXDCAUQ → 2026Q1 + unemp LRUNTTTTAUQ156S → 2026Q1; CA real GDP NGDPRSAXDCCAQ → 2026Q1 + CA fiscal raw → 2025Q4 (varied). The OECD-MEI IDs NAEXKP01{AU,CL,BR,CA}Q661S are FROZEN at 2023Q3 but were NOT used by the shipped panels.
  • ROW/SFA derived identities: in [A..C] the ROW stars are recomputed (not held-flat) at the tail → recomputed-identity, genuine if partner blocs genuine. SFA = recomputed debt-recursion residual.

docs.title: CN — principal offender (panel 2016Q1..2025Q4)
docs.text: Per-series genuine source ends (live FRED): - GDP_CN = CHNGDPNQDSMEI is NOMINAL GDP (proxy for real) AND genuine ends 2023Q3 → 9q WEO-backfill tail (2023Q4–2025Q4). Both proxy and synthetic. Most serious issue in [A..C]. - CPI_CN CHNCPIALLMINMEI → 2025Q1 → 3q backfill (2025Q2–Q4). - RATE_CN IR3TIB01CNM156N, REER_CN RBCNBIS, EQ_CN SPASTT01CNM661N → live to 2026Q1 but shipped FROZEN flat for 2025Q2–Q4 (stale-frozen mirror; re-pull, live data exists — 3q each synthetic). - EXP_CN XTEXVA01CNM667S, IMP_CN XTIMVA01CNM667S → live to 2026Q1; shipped genuine to 2025Q4 → KEEP. - POLRATE_CN (BIS CBPOL), HP_CN (BIS RPP): not FRED-re-pullable; series vary plausibly → needs-human-check. - CORP_CN: labelled "10Y gov-bond sovereign proxy" — proxy standing in for corporate credit → flag. - Fiscal FREV/FPEXP/EFFI/SFA: fiscal_raw/fiscal_CN.json ends 2025Q3 with revenue+nominal_gdp FROZEN (last 3q identical) and gross_debt LINEAR-extrapolated → synthetic fiscal tail (~3q). SFA tainted.

docs.title: Proxies (flag; keep but label)
docs.text:
• CR GDP_CR = "Real GDP (Industrial Production proxy)" — entirely a proxy (real IP data, genuine tail).
  • CL CORP_CL = "10Y Government Bond Yield (sovereign proxy)" — proxy for corporate credit price.
  • CN GDP_CN (nominal→real) and CN CORP_CN (sovereign→corporate) as above.

docs.title: EM-swarm macro (needs-human-check) — AR, BR, CL, CO, CR
docs.text: Origin commit e447f11: GDP from DBnomics OECD-QNA proxy, national central banks, IMF SDMX for CPI/unemp/trade/NEER. Swarm raws end 2025Q4 with genuinely-quarterly variation (realistic q/q incl. declines — inconsistent with disaggregation/held-last). Frozen FRED OECD-MEI mirrors were NOT used. Ends not re-verifiable with the FRED key → needs-human-check (lean keep). Short frozen runs to flag: GOV_AR (4q), POLRATE_AR (4q at 29), GOV_BR (4q) — may be genuine policy flats or held tails.

docs.title: Publish bottom line for [A..C]
docs.text:
• DROP/RE-SOURCE: CN GDP_CN (proxy + 9q backfill), CN CPI_CN (3q backfill), CN RATE/REER/EQ (3q stale-frozen — re-pull), CN fiscal tail.
  • FLAG proxy: CN GDP_CN, CR GDP_CR, CL CORP_CL, CN CORP_CN.
  • HUMAN-CHECK: AR/BR/CL/CO/CR EM-swarm macro; CN/CL/CR rates & BIS/national policy/house-price series.
  • KEEP verified: global commons; BIS credit (2025Q4); AT/BE/CZ/CH Eurostat macro+HICP+fiscal; AU/CA IMF-mirror GDP; ROW*/SFA derived identities.

docs.title: Appendix B — detailed sub-audit, blocs L..N (LT, LU, LV, MX, MY, NL, NO, NZ)
docs.text: Scope: the 8 BVAR blocs whose code starts in L..N — LT, LU, LV (Eurostat euro members), NL, NO (Eurostat), MX, MY, NZ (OECD/FRED + national swarm). gvar skipped per instructions.

docs.title: Headline finding (uniform across all 8 blocs): one-quarter MISLABEL, not fabricated values
docs.text:
The shipped panel for every L..N bloc is 40 quarters of genuine source data ending 2025Q4, but app/src/lib/dates.ts dates the last observation 2026Q1 (DEFAULT_LAST_HIST_DATE = 2026-01-01, with NO per-bloc override for any of these 8). i.e. the last shipped observation is dated one quarter into the future: genuine 2025Q4 data is presented under a "2026Q1" label.

This is NOT a fabricated/extrapolated value — the numbers are real source data — but the LAST OBSERVATION does not correspond to genuine 2026Q1 source data. It is a stale-frozen 2025Q4 mirror relabeled to 2026Q1. The genuine 2026Q1 figures now exist at the sources (see below) but are NOT in the shipped panel.

docs.title: How it was verified (triangulated, live sources)
docs.text:
historical_levels.json is a 40-row matrix, no embedded dates; dates.ts implies 2016Q2..2026Q1 (idx0..idx39). Back-transforming the shipped 100·log levels and comparing to genuine source rows:

  • shipped idx39 ("2026Q1") == genuine source 2025Q4, and shipped idx38 ("2025Q4") == genuine 2025Q3, for 8/8 macro variables in LT, LU, LV, NL, NO, MX, NZ (and MY — see MY note). Example matches:
  • LT GDP: shipped idx39 → 13087.86 ; live Eurostat namq_10_gdp CLV15 B1GQ LT 2025-Q4 = 13087.8 (2025-Q3 = 12845.0 = shipped idx38; live 2026-Q1 = 13051.5 is NOT shipped).
  • NO GDP: shipped idx39 → 106531.9 ; raw panel DataRaw_NO.json 2025-Q4 = 106532.9.
  • MX GDP: shipped idx39 → 6.4204e12 ; raw panel 2025-Q4 = 6.42047e12.
  • NZ GDP: shipped idx39 → 70579.9 ; swarm source oecd_swarm/DataRaw_NZ_dom.json last row (2025-Q4) = 70577.7.
  • Global columns (GLB_OIL/GLB_USLR/GLB_USBAA/GLB_USEQ) are ALSO shifted by one quarter — confirmed against a LIVE FRED pull (key 8ce324...):
  • WTISPLC q-avg: 2025Q3 65.737, 2025Q4 59.640, 2026Q1 71.977. Shipped GLB_OIL idx38→65.74, idx39→59.64 (= genuine 2025Q4); the genuine 2026Q1 71.977 is NOT shipped.
  • DGS10: 2025Q4 4.10, 2026Q1 4.20 → shipped GLB_USLR idx39 = 4.10 (2025Q4).
  • BAA: 2025Q4 5.83, 2026Q1 5.91 → shipped GLB_USBAA idx39 = 5.83 (2025Q4).
  • Source panels confirm 2025Q4 end: pipeline/country_rawdata/DataRaw_<CC>.json ends 2025-Q4 for LT/LU/LV/NL/NO/MX/NZ; DataRaw_<CC>_glob.json ends 2025-Q4 for all; swarm *_dom.json ends 2025-Q4. refresh-quarter.mjs only has a US source registry and is fail-closed for every other bloc (lines 189-191, 460-464) — so none of these 8 were genuinely refreshed to 2026Q1.

docs.title: Internal consistency
docs.text: Because macro, global, and derived columns are ALL shifted by the same one quarter, the panel is internally coherent (no cross-column date seam). The defect is purely the dates.ts label.

docs.title: Recommended remedy (human/code action — do NOT drop real data)
docs.text: The genuine data should be KEPT and RE-DATED to 2025Q4 (set a LAST_HIST_DATE override of 2025-10-01 for each of LT, LU, LV, MX, MY, NL, NO, NZ), OR the panel should be genuinely refreshed to 2026Q1 by adding a source registry for these blocs (the genuine 2026Q1 source data now exists). Until one of those is done, the publish should stay HALTED: the chart presents 2025Q4 data as 2026Q1. Because the values themselves are genuine, the right per-variable action is needs-human-check (re-date), NOT truncate/drop — truncating would discard a real observation. truncate-tail-to-genuine is recommended ONLY for genuinely-null/earlier-ending tails (LENDR_MX).

docs.title: Per-bloc / per-variable notes
docs.text:
• Macro real-activity & prices (GDP/CONS/INV/GOV/EXP/IMP/CPI|HICP, LR, rates, NEER/HPI, POLRATE, SPREAD, YIELD10): genuine source end 2025Q4; last shipped obs (idx39) mislabeled 2026Q1. syntheticType = stale-frozen-mirror (1 mislabeled tail quarter). dropRecommendation needs-human-check (re-date to 2025Q4, do not drop the real obs).
  • GLB_OIL / GLB_USLR / GLB_USBAA / GLB_USEQ (FRED WTISPLC/DGS10/BAA/equity): same one-quarter mislabel — verified against live FRED. Genuine end 2025Q4. stale-frozen-mirror, needs-human-check.
  • CREDIT_GDP_ (BIS via FRED, % of GDP): moves at the tail (not held); inherits the 2025Q4→2026Q1 mislabel. Genuine end 2025Q4. stale-frozen-mirror, needs-human-check.
  • LENDR_: ECB MIR (euro members), IMF IFS FILR (MX/MY/NO/NZ). Most end at the 2025Q4-labeled -2026Q1 tail. LENDR_MX is the exception: genuine IMF IFS FILR ends earlier — last real value sits at idx37 (genuine 2025Q2 = 9.14); idx38 and idx39 are NULL. So LENDR_MX has a genuine null tail on top of the mislabel → truncate-tail-to-genuine (the nulls are honest; no fabrication, but the column is ~1.5q shorter than the macro).
  • Fiscal block (LT, LU, LV, NL): FREV_GDP, FPEXP_GDP, EFFI, SFA — Eurostat gov_10q_ggnfa / gov_10q_ggdebt, 4q-trailing-sum ratios; EFFI = 400·interest/lagged-debt; SFA = recomputed debt-recursion residual (an identity, recomputed-identity). Live Eurostat gov_10q for LT publishes TR through 2025-Q4 (9169.1), so fiscal genuine end aligns with macro 2025Q4 (no forward-fill at the tail for LT). Same one-quarter mislabel. FREV/FPEXP/EFFI: stale-frozen-mirror, needs-human-check. SFA: recomputed-identity (derived by construction; also COVID-NaN'd 2020 — that is missing, not synthetic), needs-human-check.
  • ROWEA_GDP_ / ROWEA_HICP_ (euro members) and ROWDEM/ROWINF/ROWFIN_ (MX/MY/NO/NZ): GVAR-derived trade-weighted partner aggregates, NOT a single pulled series. They MOVE at the tail (not held-last), so they are derived-but-genuine through the 2025Q4 panel; they inherit the mislabel. Flag as proxy/derived (proxy-variable) with the same one-quarter stale tail. needs-human-check.
  • EACORP (shared EA IG corporate yield, ECB all-bonds 10Y): single shared external column; same mislabel; stale-frozen-mirror, needs-human-check.

docs.title: MY note (only bloc whose raw panel actually reaches 2026Q1)
docs.text: DataRaw_MY.json genuinely ends 2026-Q1 (the macro source has it). BUT the shipped MY historical_levels still uses the 2025Q4-labeled-2026Q1 row — verified: shipped MY idx39 matches the raw 2025Q4 values (CONS 267533 = raw 2025Q4 267535, not raw 2026Q1 269083; CPI 135.23 = raw 2025Q4, not raw 2026Q1 136.03; NEER, INV, GOV, EXP, IMP all match 2025Q4). So MY is one quarter STALE relative to its own available genuine 2026Q1 data, AND mislabeled. Same remedy: re-date to 2025Q4 or rebuild to the genuine 2026Q1 the source already publishes.

docs.title: What is NOT flagged
docs.text:
• COVID mask (2020Q2–2021Q1 NaN): MISSING data (handled by the COVID-as-missing Gibbs), NOT synthetic — not flagged per the directive.
  • POLRATE held tails (e.g. POLRATE_LT/LU/LV/NL idx39==idx38, MY LR/RATE/POLRATE/HP): these are EXACT carry-forwards but they reflect a policy rate / index that genuinely did not change between the two quarters (the rate was unchanged), so they are not fabrication — they are still the 2025Q4-labeled -2026Q1 mislabel like every other column.

docs.title: Verifiability caveats
docs.text: All macro and global columns were re-pulled and matched against LIVE sources (Eurostat SDMX, FRED API) or the in-repo genuine source/swarm panels. Fiscal genuine end re-pulled live for LT (gov_10q_ggnfa) and confirmed 2025Q4. The remaining per-member fiscal/credit/lending columns were not each re-pulled live against their primary API within scope; they sit on the SAME panel and inherit the SAME 2025Q4 end + one-quarter mislabel, but where a column's primary source was not independently re-pulled it is marked needs-human-check rather than assumed clean.

docs.title: Workstream-2 Feasibility — Genuine-Only Re-estimation of CN/KR/RU/TH/ID/IN (READ-ONLY)
docs.text:
Probe date 2026-06-23. No data or code edited — this is a read-only feasibility. Genuine ends were re-derived LIVE from the actual upstream sources (FRED key 8ce324…, OECD/IMF/BIS/Eurostat SDMX, BoK ECOS, national CBs via WebFetch) — the shipped 2025Q4 tail was NOT trusted. Anything not live-verified this run is flagged unverifiable / needs-human-check (never assumed real).

UPDATE 2026-06 — CN GDP_CN DONE (CN-only). This doc's headline CN item — GDP_CN is nominal (FRED CHNGDPNQDSMEI) mislabeled real — has been resolved by a surgical CN-only re-estimate: GDP_CN is now genuine real GDP (OECD QNA / NBS chain-linked SA volume index, 2011Q1+ genuine, 1999–2010 genuine annual real growth Denton-disaggregated). Only the CN bloc was re-estimated and grafted; the other 32 blocs are byte-identical. The KR/RU/TH/ID/IN re-source items below are still open. References to CN GDP as "nominal mislabeled real" below are superseded for the GDP series.

Context: MFM is live in production (main bf8c175). The synthetic-data audit (docs/synthetic-data-audit.md) found 6 blocs carry multi-quarter WEO-fabricated tails baked into the estimated 2025Q4 companion (so display-truncation alone is insufficient — the model was fit on fabricated data). This document answers: how genuine is each bloc, can it be re-estimated genuinely, and what the full re-estimation costs.

docs.title: 0. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY (per-bloc go/no-go)
docs.text:
No bloc is infeasible to re-estimate at its genuine end. Classification:

Bloc Genuine balanced end Synth q in panel Recommendation Can be made CURRENT? KR 2024-Q1 (97 obs) 7–8 needs-alt-source Yes → 2026Q1 via FRED mirror+OECD (one gate: OECD-QNA components) RU 2024-Q1 (77 obs) 8 needs-alt-source Only via national connectors (Rosstat/CBR) the pipeline lacks TH 2025-Q1 (89 obs) 3 re-estimate-on-genuine-end Re-source to 2026Q1 (NESDC national) or truncate ID 2025-Q1 (79 obs) 3 re-estimate-on-genuine-end Yes → 2026Q1 via OECD-QNA (except RATE/NEER) IN 2025-Q2 (55 obs) 2 re-estimate-on-genuine-end Partial (2025Q3 old-base; 2025Q4 needs MOSPI rebase splice) CN 2025-Q2 macro / 2024-Q4 fiscal (106 obs) 3 macro / ~4 fiscal needs-alt-source Yes for real-activity (OECD QNA real GDP → 2026Q1)

Go/no-go: all 6 are GO for re-estimation. None is "too-stale / recommend-drop" — every bloc retains a long genuine history and current financial/credit + globals layer. The split is between cleanly re-estimable now (TH, ID, IN — truncate the 2–3 synthetic q to a within-shipped genuine end and re-estimate) vs needs-alt-source to reach current (KR, RU, CN — the cheap path is still just truncate-and-re-estimate; reaching 2026Q1 needs connectors the pipeline does not have wired).

RU is the hard case (genuine 2024-Q1, no FRED activity mirror, COVID hole in a 77-obs panel) and IN is the prior-dominated case (T=55, 0.78 obs/coef — but its shortness is intrinsic, not truncation-induced).

docs.title: CN REAL-GDP verdict (the principal defect)
docs.text: The shipped GDP_CN is NOMINAL GDP (FRED CHNGDPNQDSMEI) mislabeled "real" — the spec name is literally "CN Nominal GDP (quarterly)" — AND its genuine FRED tail ends 2023Q3, with 2023Q4…2025Q4 (9q) IMF-WEO-backfilled nominal baked into the companion. This is the China real-GDP trap in full force. A genuine quarterly REAL-GDP series DOES exist and is current to 2026Q1 (verified live): OECD QNA on DBnomics, OECD/DSD_NAMAIN1@DF_QNA_EXPENDITURE_GROWTH_G20, Q.Y.CHN..B1GQ..G1.T0102 (QoQ=1.3%) / ..GY.T0102 (YoY=5.0%) for 2026Q1, sourced from NBS (official, released 2026-04-16). A real-GDP level index can be chained from the QoQ growth — the cleanest honest real-activity replacement. Recommended option: re-source GDP_CN as honestly-labelled REAL GDP (level reconstructed from OECD/NBS QoQ growth), not the nominal proxy. Do NOT use FRED PRCNTO01CNQ661S (quarterly IP) as the proxy — it is frozen at 2024Q1 (discontinued OECD-MEI mirror). With real GDP re-sourced the CN macro core lifts to 2025Q1 (CPI-bound) / 2024Q4 (fiscal-bound).

docs.title: CORP_* sovereign-proxy fix
docs.text: CN, CL, IN CORP_* are sovereign yields mislabeled as a corporate-credit price. Verified live: CORP_CN = OECD China 10Y benchmark GOVERNMENT bond yield (current to 2026Q1); CORP_IN = OECD India 10Y GOVERNMENT yield (current to 2026Q1, and conceptually duplicates YIELD10_IN). No genuine CN/IN corporate-yield series exists on OECD/FRED/BIS. The data is genuine and current; only the concept label is wrong. Fix: either keep with an explicit sovereign-proxy disclosure (the spec name already says "sovereign proxy") or drop the corp-price column for these blocs. By contrast CORP_KR is a TRUE corporate yield (BoK ECOS 3Y AA-, verified live to 2026-05) — the coverage_manifest.json source string mislabels it; the fetch code is authoritative. KR is NOT in the sovereign-proxy trap.

docs.title: GVAR panel-balance impact + estimator design
docs.text: The WEO-balancing is a convenience (rectangular per-bloc matrix), NOT an estimator requirement. cgl_bvarV5.m fits GLP coefficients only on xx(1:lastFull,:) (the last fully-observed row, line 39); remNaNs_prev method 6 leaves trailing NaNs and the DK simulation smoother imputes them — so a bloc truncated to genuine-end + NaN-padded is estimated exactly as single-country to that end (same mechanism as the COVID mask). The GVAR-EM star-completion (buildstars) renormalises trade weights over the partners present each quarter, so a partner ending 2024Q1 just drops from the star sums for later quarters. Two designs: (A) ragged-in-place (smoother extrapolates the short tail into partners' stars — flag as model-implied) vs (B) single-country to genuine end + couple at solve-time (preferred for RU/KR — no fabricated foreign feed). Recommended mix: re-source KR+ID to 2026Q1 (FRED/OECD), truncate RU/TH/IN/CN to genuine end via (B).

docs.title: Re-estimation PLAN + time
docs.text:
Order forced: re-source/truncate → re-estimate (all blocs) → re-export → coupling regen (MANDATORY) → verify → ship. 1. Re-source / truncate — KR+ID→2026Q1 (cheap FRED/OECD); RU/TH/IN/CN truncate to genuine end (drop WEO ext); CN drop/re-source GDP_CN nominal→real, truncate fiscal→2024Q4: 0.5–1.5 d (national re-source is the long pole; cheapest honest variant = just disable WEO extension → ~2 h). 2. Re-estimate the WHOLE GVAR (US anchor + 32 RoW — all re-estimated because every star changes): export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m, 1000 draws: 8–16 h unattended (the compute floor). 3. Re-export per-bloc JSON contract: ~5 min. 4. Coupling regen (MANDATORY, FULL) — any of the 6 changing flips the global modelSig and invalidates the entire shipped coupling_default.json.gz → without regen the browser falls back to the ~19-min in-browser (I−M)⁻¹ build. Rebuild M (all blocs) + BLAS (I−M)⁻¹ + re-gzip 32 MB: ~20–25 min. Confirm US topology (frozen-anchor live vs US-endo staging) first. 5. Verify — artifact==live, convergence/coherence, deterministic-mean + gt.m canary, CF==iterative <1e-4, spot-check 6 central paths: 0.5–1 d.

End-to-end ≈ 2–3.5 calendar days, dominated by the RU/TH/IN/CN non-FRED re-source decision and the 8–16 h re-estimation (the floor), not the coupling regen.

docs.title: Explicit unverifiable / needs-human-check items
docs.text:
• RU/TH/IN/CN genuine ends rest on non-FRED EM-swarm sources (CBR/Rosstat, NESDC, MOSPI, NBS via DBnomics/IMF-SDMX); their NGDPRSAXDC{RU,TH,IN,CN}Q FRED mirrors all 404 — the un-extended raw end is the honest floor; pushing past it is needs-human-check.
  • LENDR_TH, LENDR_ID — IMF SDMX (IMF.STA,MFS_IR/legacy IFS) returned empty/404 this run; columns move (plausibly genuine) but vintage end NOT independently confirmed — do not assume real.
  • KR OECD-QNA expenditure components (CONS/INV/GOV/EXP/IMP) — dataflow key 404'd this run; the one gap blocking a fully-current KR rebuild. Human must pin the OECD-QNA expenditure dataflow.
  • IN MOSPI rebase — 2025Q4 real GDP exists only under the new 2022-23 base (Feb-2026); a clean old-base splice reaches only 2025Q3 — the base-year bridge is needs-human-check.
  • CN POLRATE_CN — last two quarters identical at 3.0; plausibly a genuine policy hold, verify the BIS vintage end before keeping.
  • All ROW*_C stars (every bloc) — derived trade-weighted partner aggregates; genuine only insofar as partners are genuine; must be recomputed after re-source. Unverifiable in isolation.
  • NEER/scale mismatches (RU IMF-NEER ~52.7 vs BIS ~99–105; KR LR 2.8 vs 3.29; KR/RU/IN re-source is a full-column SWAP not a tail-append — a splice would inject a level break).
  • US topology for the coupling regen — frozen-anchor live vs US-endogenous staging (not deployed); confirm before regen (different coupling operators/dims).

Detailed per-bloc probes (CN, KR, RU, TH, ID, IN) and the full GVAR-panel feasibility follow.

docs.title: Notable corrections to the manifest / audit
docs.text:
• coverage_manifest.json labels CORP_KR "national 10Y government/sovereign-proxy yield" — WRONG. The fetch code (pipeline/fetch_credit_kr.py) + spec name = BoK ECOS 3Y AA- corporate bond yield, a TRUE corporate yield (verified live to 2026-05). KR is NOT in the CN/CL/IN sovereign-proxy trap.
  • Audit said "RATE/YIELD10 held-last (no WEO)". Confirmed: held-flat from 2024Q1 (7 quarters).

docs.title: KR genuine BALANCED end (core macro jointly genuine)
docs.text: 2024Q1. The 10 domestic-block macro/rate vars (GDP,CONS,INV,GOV,EXP,IMP,CPI,LR,RATE,YIELD10) are jointly genuine only through 2024Q1; 2024Q2..2025Q4 (7 q) is WEO-extension (activity/CPI) + held-last (LR/RATE/YIELD10). POLRATE/HP/CREDIT/CORP and the globals run genuinely to 2025Q4+.

docs.title: Recommendation: needs-alt-source
docs.text:
NOT drop-bloc — the bloc has a long genuine history and its financial block + globals are current. NOT a plain re-estimate-on-2024Q1 either: that needlessly discards 7 quarters when GENUINE current data EXISTS for the stale series: - GDP_KR → FRED NGDPRSAXDCKRQ (2026Q1, live) - CPI_KR → OECD SDMX prices (2026-05, live) - RATE_KR → FRED IR3TIB01KRM156N; YIELD10_KR → IRLTLT01KRM156N; LR_KR → LRUNTTTTKRQ156S (all live) - CONS/INV/GOV/EXP/IMP_KR → OECD-QNA expenditure (publishes 2026Q1; NOT live-verified this run — dataflow key needs a human to pin; this is the ONE gap blocking a fully-current rebuild)

Two caveats a human must resolve before re-sourcing: 1. Level/concept mismatch. FRED IMF-mirror GDP and FRED harmonized unemployment are at DIFFERENT levels than the swapped-out swarm series (e.g. LR ~2.8 vs shipped 3.29). Re-sourcing is a series SWAP, not a tail-append — it changes the full column scale and must be rebuilt end-to-end, not spliced (a splice at 2024Q1 would inject a level break). 2. OECD-QNA components are the one un-live-verified piece. If a human can pull OECD-QNA expenditure to 2026Q1, KR re-builds cleanly to a genuine 2026Q1/2025Q4 end. If not, the honest fallback is re-estimate-on-genuine-end = 2024Q1 (truncate the 7 synthetic q), keeping the financial block genuine.

Bottom line: needs-alt-source (preferred: live re-source to 2025Q4/2026Q1 via FRED/OECD/BIS/ECOS, gated on confirming OECD-QNA components); re-estimate-on-2024Q1 is the safe fallback. The GVAR stars must be regenerated whichever path, since they are contaminated by partner-bloc synthetic tails.

docs.title: TH — dating verification
docs.text:
Shipped app/public/data/bvar/TH/historical_levels.json = 40 rows. Back-transforming GDP_TH (100·log) and matching the GVAR-EM swarm raw panel pipeline/oecd_swarm/DataRaw_TH_dom.json (2003Q1..2025Q1) + the estimation-panel builder output pipeline/country_rawdata/DataRaw_TH_2025q4.json: - shipped row 36 == genuine swarm 2025-Q1 (GDP 2845360≈2845342, CPI/CONS/INV exact match rows 30–36). - shipped rows 37/38/39 == 2025-Q2/Q3/Q4, NOT in the swarm source → build_gvar_2025q4.py WEO-fabricated (activity/prices) + held-last (rates/NEER). So for TH the last shipped row is the WEO target 2025-Q4 (NOT a 2026-Q1 off-by-one). Synthetic tail = 3 quarters — correcting the audit's "genuine ends 2025Q2 / 4q"; the swarm source definitively ends 2025-Q1.

These 3 fabricated quarters entered the 2025-Q4 estimation panel (build_gvar_2025q4.py WEO-balances before re-estimation; refresh-quarter.mjs keeps the companion FROZEN, no TH registry, fail-closed). The estimated companion saw fabricated activity/price + held-flat rate/NEER at the final 3 quarters → display-truncation NOT sufficient.

ROW* stars must be regenerated (contaminated by partner tails).

The China real-GDP trap. 2. CPI WEO-backfilled past 2025Q1; RATE/REER/EQ frozen-flat for 3q in the shipped panel though genuine FRED data runs to 2026Q1; the fiscal block is annual data held-flat within each year (no genuine quarterly fiscal) with gross_debt linearly interpolated (+3253/q in 2024, +3862/q in 2025).

docs.title: RU bloc probe (READ-ONLY)
docs.text:
RU is a deep-synthesis Tier-1 bloc. The original DataRaw_RU.json input panel hard-truncates ALL columns at 2024-Q1 (origin commit e447f11: RU real GDP from World Bank GEM "since OECD/IMF discontinued RUS GDP post-2022 — capped honestly at 2024Q1, no fabrication"; CPI/unemployment/trade/NEER from IMF-SDMX). build_gvar_2025q4.py then WEO-extended GDP/CPI/EXP and held-last LR/RATE/NEER for 8 quarters (2024Q2–2026Q1) to balance the panel → these 8 synthetic quarters ARE baked into companion_mean/draws, so display-truncation alone is insufficient.

KEY SPLIT (verified live): the financial layer is fully refreshable TODAY via wired sources; the 6 core macro vars are STUCK at 2024-Q1 in the wired pipeline because IMF discontinued Russian official stats post-2022. Genuine current data DOES exist but only at NATIONAL sources the pipeline is not wired to (Rosstat GDP −0.2% y/y Q1-2026 rel. 17-Jun-2026; Rosstat CPI 5.9% Mar / unemployment 2.2% Apr-2026; CBR MIACR/RUONIA 13.75% Jun-2026; CBR-BoP/FCS goods exports $98.3bn Q1-2026; BIS WS_EER NEER to 2026Q2).

docs.title: ID bloc probe (READ-ONLY)

HOWEVER the synthesis was NOT necessary — verified live that genuine current data exists: OECD QNA SDMX (DSD_NAMAIN1@DF_QNA, IDN, T0102 expenditure) publishes SA real GDP + components through 2026-Q1 (2025Q4 growth 5.30%, 2026Q1 5.46%); OECD prices SDMX (IDN CPI all-items IX) is genuine monthly through 2026-05. The financial/credit block is genuine and current as shipped.

docs.title: GVAR PANEL + RE-ESTIMATION FEASIBILITY (read-only)
docs.text: Probe date 2026-06-23. No data/code edited. Assesses whether the ~33-bloc GVAR can be re-estimated with the six WEO-contaminated blocs (CN/KR/RU/TH/ID/IN) truncated to their genuine ends: what the production estimator (cgl_bvarV5.m) does on a short/ragged panel, the coupling-regen blast radius, and a sequencing + time estimate. Genuine ends re-derived from the actual sources (not the shipped tail); unverifiable items flagged.

docs.title: 0. Genuine ends — re-derived (the panel actually fed to MATLAB is DataRaw_<C>_glob.json, WEO-balanced)
docs.text:
The estimation panel is DataRaw_<C>_glob.json (globals-augmented, ends 2025-Q4 for all = WEO-balanced). The un-extended DataRaw_<C>.json exposes the genuine end:

Bloc genuine end (DataRaw_<C>.json) genuine T synth q in panel FRED real-GDP mirror to 2026Q1? KR 2024-Q1 97 7–8 YES NGDPRSAXDCKRQ→2026Q1 (verified); rate IR3TIB01KRM156N→2026Q1 RU 2024-Q1 77 8 NO (NGDPRSAXDCRUQ 404); rate IRSTCB01RUM156N froze 2023Q3 — non-FRED, needs-human-check TH 2025-Q1 89 3 NO (NGDPRSAXDCTHQ 404); NESDC national — needs-human-check ID 2025-Q1 79 3 YES NGDPRSAXDCIDQ→2026Q1 (verified) IN 2025-Q2 55 2 NO (NGDPRSAXDCINQ 404); MOSPI national — needs-human-check CN 2025-Q2 macro / 2024-Q4 fiscal 106 3 macro / ~4 fiscal GDP_CN=nominal mislabeled real (drop/re-source); RATE/REER/EQ re-pullable→2026Q1

KR/RU = 2024-Q1 confirmed (the worst cases). KR and ID can be brought CURRENT (FRED IMF-mirror real GDP live to 2026Q1) rather than truncated — removes them from the ragged set. RU/TH/IN/CN activity have no FRED mirror (EM-swarm: CBR/Rosstat, NESDC, MOSPI, national CB via DBnomics/ IMF-SDMX); their genuine ends are needs-human-check and the un-extended raw end is the honest floor.

docs.title: 1. Can the GVAR be re-estimated with the 6 truncated? — YES; the estimator handles ragged natively
docs.text:
The estimator already drops a trailing NaN block (verified in cgl_bvarV5.m): - lastFull = find(~isnan(sum(x,2)),1,'last') (line 39) → the GLP coefficient fit uses only xx(1:lastFull,:) (lines 54, 162-163, 183). A trailing NaN block is excluded from the likelihood. - remNaNs_prev method 6 (Support/remNaNs_prev.m:135-146) carries previous values only between each column's first/last obs (t1:t2); trailing NaNs after a column's last obs are LEFT NaN and the DK simulation smoother (Xcond, lines 121-205) imputes them; the forecast splice starts at full T (lines 195-197). So a bloc truncated to its genuine end + NaN-padded to the common grid is estimated exactly as single-country to that end, with the smoother extrapolating the pad. Same mechanism the COVID mask uses — not a new code path.

Does GVAR-EM / DK star-completion handle a 2024-Q1 end (KR/RU, 7–8q behind)? Yes, by construction. buildstars (export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m:177-205) intersects partner quarters per period (tp=find(D.(pn).dates==q,1)) and renormalises trade weights over the partners present each quarter (wd/wi/wf accumulate only available partners). A partner ending 2024-Q1 simply drops out of every star sum for 2024Q2–2025Q4; the rest renormalise. No balanced-panel requirement at the star layer — build_gvar_2025q4.py WEO-balances purely for a rectangular per-bloc matrix, not an estimator constraint.

Two viable designs: - (A) Ragged-in-place: drop the WEO extension, feed genuine-then-NaN; the EM loop's buildstars renormalises per quarter; the DK smoother extrapolates each short bloc's tail. Risk: RU/KR 2024Q2–2025Q4 becomes pure smoother extrapolation propagated into other blocs' stars (a model-implied, not observed, foreign block for 7–8q). Acceptable only if flagged as model-implied. - (B) Single-country to genuine end, coupled at solve-time (preferred for RU/KR): estimate each short bloc to its own dates, build stars over the partner overlap. No fabricated foreign feed; the honest ragged edge. Already how dates are keyed per bloc.

Net: re-estimable with all 6 truncated. Preferred mix — re-source KR + ID to 2026Q1 (FRED mirror), truncate RU/TH/IN/CN to genuine end via (B). RU is the only genuinely short/early case.

docs.title: 2. cgl_bvarV5.m on a short/ragged panel + min-obs
docs.text:
Production call: cgl_bvarV5(x, 3, statCols, 1000, 500, 1, 20, 'mcmc',1,'MNpsi',0,'sur',0,'noc',1, ...). p=3 lags, H=20, 1000/500 draws. COVID = FIXED 2020Q2–Q4 (macro+ROWDEM/ROWINF NaN'd, financials+ROWFIN observed). No hard min-T / df assertion in bvarGLPmf — GLP is Bayesian-Minnesota, identified even when (3n+1) > (T−p) (prior-dominated, not infeasible). sur=0 is correct (matches the US requirement).

Parameter/obs pressure at the genuine end (n includes the 3 stars; p=3):

Bloc n genuine T eff rows (T−p) coefs/eq (3n+1) obs-per-coef CN 22 106 103 67 1.54 KR 21 97 94 64 1.47 RU 17 77 74 52 1.42 TH 22 89 86 67 1.28 ID 20 79 76 61 1.25 IN 22 55 52 67 0.78

IN is the binding bloc (0.78 obs/coef — fewer effective rows than coefficients/equation): estimable but heavily prior-shrunk, OOS tail ≈ prior RW → flag low-information. Its shortness is intrinsic (panel starts 2011-Q4), not truncation-induced (drops only 2q). RU (1.42, drops 8q) is the data-light early-ending case; with the 3-q COVID hole still masked it leaves ~71 effective likelihood rows — path (B) avoids the additional forward extrapolation. No bloc is infeasible; flag IN and RU as prior-dominated.

docs.title: 3. Coupling regen scope — FULL regen; the ~19-min M build is unavoidable
docs.text:
Each of the 6 feeds the stars of ~31–32 of the 33 blocs (verified vs weights_full.json): CN→32, ID→31, IN→32, KR→32, RU→32, TH→32. Truncating/re-sourcing any one changes its completed comp panel → every partner's buildstars → every partner's estimated companion → M for essentially ALL blocs. Not a local edit: - M is data-keyed. precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs keys the artifact to modelSig = gvarSignature(raw) over the deployed bloc data; it hydrates iff modelSig matches. Any change to any of the 6 blocs' deployed companion_mean/historical_levels/baseline_levels flips modelSig → invalidates the shipped coupling_default.json.gz for the WHOLE GVAR. A stale-sig artifact is ignored → browser falls back to the ~19-min in-browser (I−M)⁻¹ build (unacceptable). - So the artifact MUST be regenerated (build M in JS via DK-smoother probes → MATLAB BLAS (I−M)⁻¹ ~1s → re-emit 32 MB gzip). Mandatory, not optional (GVAR-coupling-precache memo). - US-topology wrinkle: the precompute branches on US_ENDOGENOUS (buildGVARCouplingMEndo at larger dim) vs frozen-anchor buildGVARCouplingM. Confirm which is LIVE (US-endo staging at pipeline/gvar_usendo_staging is not deployed per memory) before regen.

Bottom line: a FULL GVAR coupling regen. The (I−M)⁻¹ inverse is offloaded to BLAS (~1s); the M build (3·H·#blocs DK-smoother probes, all blocs) is the ~19-min piece.

docs.title: 4. Sequencing + realistic time estimate
docs.text:
Order is forced (M is built from the new companions; the EM loop couples all blocs through stars): re-source/re-truncate → re-estimate (all blocs) → re-export → coupling regen → verify.

# Step Tooling Time 1 Re-source KR+ID→2026Q1 (FRED mirror+rate); truncate RU/TH/IN/CN to genuine end (drop WEO ext); CN: drop/re-source GDP_CN (nominal→real), re-pull RATE/REER/EQ, truncate fiscal→2024Q4 edit build_gvar_2025q4.py to skip extension / rebuild _glob 0.5–1.5 d (RU/TH/IN/CN national re-source is the long pole, needs-human-check) 2 Re-estimate WHOLE GVAR (US anchor + 32 RoW; all re-estimated b/c stars change); 1000 draws, MAXIT≤12, TOL=0.03, parfor export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m 8–16 h wall (the validated full-run cost) 3 Re-export per-bloc JSON contract; save .mat (cheap re-export on a bug) exportbloc → /tmp/gvar24_export → stage into app/public/data/bvar/<C> ~5 min 4 Coupling regen (MANDATORY) — rebuild M (all blocs) + BLAS (I−M)⁻¹ + re-gzip 32 MB; new modelSig (confirm US-endo vs anchor first) precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs ~20–25 min (~19-min M build dominates) 5 Verify: gvar-coupling-artifact.test.ts (artifact==live), convergence/coherence tests, deterministic-mean + gt.m canary, CF==iterative <1e-4; spot-check 6 central paths (RU/IN prior-dominated edge expected) jest + MATLAB canary 0.5–1 d

End-to-end ~2–3.5 calendar days, dominated by (1) the RU/TH/IN/CN non-FRED re-source decision (or accept truncation) and (2) the 8–16 h unattended GVAR-EM re-estimation — the compute floor, not the coupling regen. Cheapest honest variant: skip national re-sourcing, just disable WEO extension for the 6 in build_gvar_2025q4.py (KR+ID still cheap-resourced from FRED to 2026Q1), re-run 2–5; cuts step 1 to ~2 h.

docs.title: 5. Flags (not assumed real)
docs.text:
• RU (worst): genuine 2024-Q1, no FRED mirror, COVID hole in a 77-obs panel → path (B); its 2024Q2–2025Q4 foreign feed is absent (not extrapolated) under (B). National end needs-human-check.
  • IN: T=55, 0.78 obs/coef → prior-dominated; drops only 2q (shortness is intrinsic).
  • CN GDP_CN nominal mislabeled real = drop-or-re-source, not truncation. panelfrom already deflates CN GDP by subtracting the price column (loop:173) so CN's star contribution is real-GDP-correct, but the bloc's own GDP forecast stays a nominal proxy.
  • TH genuine end re-derived to 2025-Q1 (raw panel), not the audit's 2025-Q2 — re-confirm at NESDC.
  • Coupling modelSig is global — any of the 6 changing invalidates the entire shipped artifact → full regen + 32 MB re-ship; no partial coupling update exists.
  • US topology unconfirmed for the regen (frozen-anchor live vs US-endo staging) — confirm before step 4.

No bloc is infeasible to re-estimate at its genuine end. The two real constraints are (i) the non-FRED re-source decision for RU/TH/IN/CN (or accept truncation + flagged ragged edge) and (ii) the mandatory full coupling regen. RU and IN are the prior-dominated blocs to watch.

docs.title: Workstream-2 Re-stage Status — Genuine-Only Re-source of CN/KR/ID/TH/IN + RU-Drop Plan
docs.text:
Date 2026-06-25. STAGING + PLANNING ONLY — no production changes. This document captures the state after the genuine-only re-source of the 5 contaminated EM blocs the user chose to KEEP (CN, KR, ID, TH, IN) and the plan to DROP RU. It is the bridge between the read-only feasibility (docs/workstream2-feasibility.md) / audit (docs/synthetic-data-audit.md) and an eventual re-estimation + ship.

Hard constraints honoured (NO-SYNTHETIC is ABSOLUTE): every series was re-derived from its GENUINE upstream source; any quarter that could not be genuinely sourced is left honestly null and flagged needs-human-check — NO WEO-fill, held-last, or interpolation anywhere. All staged output is under pipeline/ws2_staging/<C>/ (gitignored, line 48 of .gitignore). app/public/data is untouched, no MATLAB was run, no coupling was regenerated, nothing was committed.

docs.title: 1. RU-DROP CHECKLIST (enumerated; DO NOT APPLY)
docs.text: The user chose to DROP RU (genuine macro core stuck at 2024-Q1; no FRED activity mirror; reaching current needs Rosstat/CBR/FCS connectors the pipeline lacks — see feasibility §RU). Removing RU from the live system is the following precise, exhaustive set of edits. Nothing here has been applied.

docs.title: 1.1 App source / TypeScript
docs.text:
1. app/src/lib/data/countries.ts — line 61: delete the entry { code: 'RU', name: 'Russia', group: 'em' },. This drops RU from COUNTRIES, and therefore from the derived EM_COUNTRIES filter and from the dropdown (see §1.4 — the dropdown is data-driven off this list, so no separate dropdown edit is needed).

  2. app/src/lib/compute/gvarSolve.ts — line 57: remove 'RU' from the CountryCode string-union type (… | 'BR' | 'RU' | 'ID' | …). Type-only; removing it makes any stray 'RU' literal a compile error (a useful tripwire that catches leftovers).

  3. NOTE: GVAR_BLOC_CANDIDATES (line 97) is NOT a hardcoded list — it is WEIGHTS24.blocs, derived at import time from weights_24bloc.json. So the operative bloc-list edit is the weights file (§1.2), NOT a code edit. Likewise ROW_COUNTRIES/COUPLED_COUNTRIES/IDX/W are populated by configureGVARTopology() from the weights + per-bloc spec.json presence; they auto-drop RU once the weights row/cols and the RU data dir are gone.

docs.title: 1.2 Trade-weight files (the operative bloc-list + the star renormalisation)
docs.text:
RU is BOTH a bloc (origin row) and a partner (column) in the weight matrices. It must be removed as a bloc entry, as an origin key, and as a partner key under EVERY other origin, then the remaining rows must be renormalised (each origin's partner weights must re-sum to 1 over the surviving partners) — otherwise the GVAR stars are mis-weighted.

  1. app/public/data/bvar/gvar/weights_24bloc.json (THE LIVE FILE — gvarSolve.ts imports it):
  2. remove "RU" from the blocs array (currently 33 entries → 32).
  3. remove "RU" from bloc_codes ("RU": "RUS").
  4. remove the "RU" origin key from weights (RU's own partner row).
  5. remove "RU" as a partner key from all 32 other origins' weight dicts (verified: RU appears as a partner under 32 origins).

  6. RENORMALISE each of the 32 surviving origins' partner weights to sum to 1 after RU's column is dropped. (Functionally equivalent to what buildstars does at estimation time when a partner is absent — but the shipped weights file must be made consistent for the in-browser solver.)

  7. app/public/data/bvar/gvar/weights_full.json (full 33-bloc reference; not the live import but shipped + used by tests/topology checks): same 5 sub-edits + renormalise. RU appears 35× (blocs array, bloc_codes, origin key, 32 partner cols).

  8. app/public/data/bvar/gvar/weights_49.json (50-bloc superset; RU 50×): same removal + renormalise IF this file is ever promoted to live; otherwise drop RU for consistency.

  9. Back-ups (optional, for hygiene — not load-bearing): app/public/data/bvar/gvar/weights_24bloc.json.bak24, app/public/data/bvar/gvar/weights_full_50bloc_backup.json — leave as historical backups OR strip RU to avoid a future accidental restore re-introducing RU. Recommend: leave untouched, document.

docs.title: 1.3 RU data directory (per-bloc model artifacts)
docs.text: 1. Delete app/public/data/bvar/RU/ entirely (8 files): baseline.json, baseline_levels.json, companion_draws.json.gz, companion_mean.json, historical.json, historical_levels.json, spec.json, transformations.json. getAvailableBlocs() (gvarLoader) filters GVAR_BLOC_CANDIDATES by which raw[c] actually loaded (404-tolerant), so removing the dir alone would drop RU at runtime — but the weights file must ALSO be cleaned (§1.2) or RU stays a phantom partner column in the stars.

docs.title: 1.4 Dropdown / selector
docs.text: 1. No dedicated edit. The country dropdown (TopBar.tsx / ControlPanel.tsx / ChartGrid.tsx) renders from COUNTRIES / EM_COUNTRIES in countries.ts. Removing the RU entry (§1.1.1) removes it from the UI automatically. (Confirm no hardcoded 'RU' string literal remains in those three components — grep clean as of this writing; the type edit §1.1.2 would flag any.)

docs.title: 1.5 Default anchors / coverage manifest / generators
docs.text:
1. app/public/data/default_anchors.json: remove the "RU" anchor object (long-run slider defaults). Shipped artifact consumed by the trend/anchor layer.

  2. app/scripts/build_default_anchors.mjs — line 106: remove the RU row RU: { growth: 1.5, infl: 4.0, policy: 7.0, unemp: 4.5, mmrate: 7.0, lending: 9.0 }, so a regen does not re-introduce RU into default_anchors.json.

  3. app/public/data/coverage_manifest.json: remove the "RU" coverage entry.

  4. app/scripts/build_coverage_manifest.mjs — lines ~194/196: remove RU from the genuine-end map (KR: '2024Q1', RU: '2024Q1', …) and the explanatory comment, so a manifest regen does not re-add RU.

docs.title: 1.6 Coupling regen scope (MANDATORY downstream, not a hand-edit)
docs.text: 1. app/public/data/bvar/gvar/coupling_default.json.gz must be regenerated (it cannot be hand-edited). Dropping RU changes the bloc set and renormalises every partner's stars → flips the global modelSig → invalidates the shipped 32 MB artifact. Run precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs AFTER re-estimation (rebuild M over the surviving blocs → BLAS (I−M)⁻¹ → re-gzip). Confirm US topology (frozen-anchor live vs US-endo staging) BEFORE the regen. ~20–25 min. This is shared with the re-source path — a single regen covers both the RU drop and the 5 re-sourced blocs.

docs.title: 1.7 Tests / docs to update (to keep green-gate honest)
docs.text:
1. Tests referencing a 33-bloc count or RU explicitly: gvar-derived-topology.test.ts (loads weights_24bloc.json), any coupling-artifact / convergence test asserting bloc counts. Update expected counts 33→32 and remove RU fixtures.

  2. Docs: docs/synthetic-data-audit.md, docs/workstream2-feasibility.md, the user manual / PIPELINE docs that enumerate the bloc set — note RU dropped.

RU-drop checklist length: 15 enumerated edits across 4 strata (3 app-source, 4 weight-file groups, 1 data-dir, 5 anchor/manifest/coupling, 2 test/doc) — of which the load-bearing minimum to drop RU cleanly from the running app is 4: (a) countries.ts line 61, (b) weights_24bloc.json remove+ renormalise, (c) delete app/public/data/bvar/RU/, (d) regen coupling_default.json.gz. The rest are consistency/hygiene edits that prevent regressions and regen re-introduction.

docs.title: 2. RE-ESTIMATION READINESS — per bloc (CN/KR/ID/TH/IN)
docs.text:
The production estimator cgl_bvarV5.m handles ragged ends NATIVELY: lastFull = find(~isnan(sum(x,2)),1,'last') (line 39) → bvarGLPmf fits GLP coefficients only on xx(1:lastFull,:); remNaNs_prev method 6 + the DK simulation smoother impute trailing NaNs (same mechanism as the COVID mask). So a genuine-then-NaN ragged tail is estimated exactly as single-country to the last fully-observed row.

CRITICAL READINESS CAVEAT (load-bearing). lastFull is the last row with NO NaN in ANY column. A staged panel where the derived columns (the 3 ROW* stars, SFA) are entirely null has lastFull =(first all-null row) ≈ panel start → the GLP fit would collapse. Therefore none of the 5 staged panels is directly feed-ready as-is: the derived columns must be populated first. Two of them are populated only by the GVAR-EM estimator itself:

  • ROWDEM/ROWINF/ROWFIN_<C> (all 5 blocs): trade-weighted partner stars. NOT standalone-sourceable; export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m buildstars computes them over the genuine partner panels DURING the EM loop. They are SUPPOSED to be empty in the staged per-bloc panel — the loop fills them. So the staged panel feeds the loop (which builds stars), not cgl_bvarV5 directly.
  • SFA (CN; analogous fiscal residual logic): a recomputed debt-recursion residual (fiscalAccounting.ts computeFiscalBlock), produced app-side from the genuine fiscal inputs + observed debt/GDP. Not a pulled column. CN's genuine quarterly gross-debt input (BIS FRED QCNGANXDCA to 2025-Q4) is recorded in provenance_CN.json for that recomputation.

Net readiness model: the staged panels are the GENUINE DOMESTIC + GLOBAL columns ready to feed the GVAR-EM loop (export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m), which then (i) builds the stars and (ii) calls cgl_bvarV5 per bloc on the star-augmented panel. They are NOT a drop-in for a bare cgl_bvarV5 call (the stars would be missing). This matches the existing pipeline contract — build_gvar_2025q4.py produces DataRaw_<C>_glob.json WITHOUT stars and the loop adds them.

docs.title: Per-bloc readiness table
docs.text:
Bloc Genuine BALANCED end Macro-core end (fiscal/CPI excluded) Sourced vars needs-human-check vars Feed-ready to GVAR-EM loop? CN 2024-Q4 (fiscal-bound) 2025-Q1 (CPI-bound); 7 of 8 core reach 2026-Q1 14 sourced + 1 relabel (CORP→sovereign) CPI (2025-Q1 binding), FREV/FPEXP/EFFI (2024-Q4 WEO-annual), SFA (recompute), 3 ROW* YES once SFA recomputed + stars built by loop; macro core honestly ragged KR 2025-Q4 (2026-Q1 if 2 BIS cols NaN-padded 1q) 2026-Q1 17 sourced + 1 relabel (CORP true-corp) GLB_USEQ scale convention (level vs 100·log), 3 ROW* YES (best bloc) — full core genuine to 2026-Q1; stars by loop ID 2025-Q4 2026-Q1 (most) 14 sourced LENDR_ID (IMF SDMX empty this run), 3 ROW* YES — CPI/RATE/NEER are column SWAPs (growth-chain from 2025-Q1 seam, don't level-append); LENDR honestly null TH 2025-Q1 (macro-core jointly genuine) 2025-Q2 (real-activity only) 18 sourced + 1 relabel (GLB_USEQ transform) CPI_TH (2025-Q1; MOC rebase needs splice), GLB_USEQ model-space transform, 3 ROW* YES — financials/globals to 2025-Q4; macro to 2025-Q1 honestly ragged IN 2025-Q2 2025-Q2 (T=55, prior-dominated) 14 sourced + 1 relabel (CORP→sovereign) GDP+5 components (MOSPI 2022-23 rebase blocks 2025-Q3/Q4), CPI_IN, NEER_IN, 3 ROW* YES with caveat — 0.78 obs/coef → heavily prior-shrunk; financials to 2025-Q4; macro stuck 2025-Q2 by MOSPI rebase

Staged paths (gitignored):

  • CN: pipeline/ws2_staging/CN/ — panel_CN_genuine.json (22-var × 109-quarter 1999-Q1..2026-Q1, honest nulls), provenance_CN.json (per-var + _gross_debt_input aux = BIS FRED QCNGANXDCA to 2025-Q4).
  • KR: pipeline/ws2_staging/KR/ — KR_panel.json (2016-Q2..2026-Q1), KR_genuine_ends.json, PROVENANCE.md, resource_kr.py.
  • ID: pipeline/ws2_staging/ID/ — staged_panel.csv, staged_2025q2_q4.json, PROVENANCE.md.
  • TH: pipeline/ws2_staging/TH/ — genuine_panel.json (2016-Q1..2025-Q4), provenance.json, _manifest.json, 19 per-var CSVs, _genuine_raw.json.
  • IN: pipeline/ws2_staging/IN/ — IN_staged_panel.json / .csv (2016-Q2..2025-Q4), PROVENANCE.md, _assembled_panel.json.

docs.title: 3. EXPLICIT needs-human-check LIST (do NOT assume real)
docs.text:
Resolve each before building the estimation panels.

Derived columns — recompute by the estimator (every bloc, expected-empty): - ROWDEM/ROWINF/ROWFIN_{CN,KR,ID,TH,IN} (15 columns) — trade-weighted partner stars; built by export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m buildstars over genuine partner panels post full re-source. Cannot be sourced standalone. Left honestly null. - SFA_CN — recomputed debt-recursion residual; produce app-side via fiscalAccounting.ts on the re-sourced inputs + the genuine BIS gross-debt (QCNGANXDCA, in provenance).

Genuine-end / source blockers: - CN CPI_CN — FRED CHNCPIALLMINMEI monthly ends 2025-04 → only 2025-Q1 is a full quarter. Reaching 2025-Q4/2026-Q1 needs an alt source (OECD prices SDMX / NBS monthly). Binds the CN macro core. - CN fiscal (FREV/FPEXP/EFFI) — genuine only to 2024-Q4 (IMF WEO 2025-04 ANNUAL, flat-within-year; 2025 is a WEO PROJECTION, dropped). The exact WEO actual/forecast boundary could NOT be machine-verified (IMF datamapper 403'd). No genuine quarterly CN GG fiscal source is wired. - CN CORP_CN — relabeled 10Y SOVEREIGN (no genuine CN corporate yield exists). Keep-with-disclosure or drop the column. Same trap: IN CORP_IN (sovereign 10Y, duplicates YIELD10_IN). - KR GLB_USEQ — staged genuine S&P500 q-avg LEVEL (~6778 for 2025-Q4) vs the shipped 100·log NASDAQ-scaled convention (~202.7). Re-export must apply the SAME index/scaling convention as the live globals before splicing. - ID LENDR_ID — IMF SDMX (MFS_IR / legacy IFS FILR_PA) returned NO DATA/empty this run. Not re-derivable; left missing. - ID CPI_ID / NEER_ID / RATE_ID — column SWAPs (genuine base differs from the retired column: CPI 2015=100 vs ~173; NEER BIS-broad ~88–95 vs ~459). The builder MUST growth-chain from the genuine 2025-Q1 seam, NOT level-append (a splice injects a level break). RATE_ID confirmed == BI policy rate; human confirm the spec did not intend a distinct money-market rate. - TH CPI_TH — IFS PCPI_IX genuine to 2025-Q1; MOC current CPI is on a different base (2019=100) → rebase/splice needed (not done). TH GLB_USEQ — model-space build transform not reproducible standalone (raw SP500 genuine staged). - IN MOSPI 2022-23 rebase — GDP + 5 components cannot be genuinely extended to 2025-Q3/Q4 on the old 2011-12-base level history without a human-decided base-year bridge. IN CPI_IN (IFS freezes 2025-Q2; OECD-prices is a different-base index → splice break). IN NEER_IN (BIS-broad vintage froze 2025-Q2; live FRED mirrors a different normalization → full-column rebase).

Cross-cutting: - GLB_USEQ history/scale — pre-2016-Q2 honestly missing for some blocs (FRED SP500 10y window); fine for the 2016-Q2-start model window. Scale/convention must be harmonised with live globals. - US topology for the coupling regen — frozen-anchor (live) vs US-endogenous (staged, not deployed). Confirm BEFORE regen (different coupling operators/dims).

docs.title: 4. NEXT STEPS (sequence — forced order)
docs.text:
1. Resolve the needs-human-check items (§3): pin CN-CPI / CN-fiscal / IN-rebase / ID-LENDR alt-sources or accept the honest ragged end; confirm CORP relabels (disclose vs drop); fix GLB_USEQ scale convention; confirm US topology.
  2. Decide RU drop scope and (when approved) apply the §1 checklist — but the data/weights edits only take effect through a re-estimation + coupling regen, so batch RU-drop WITH the re-source.
  3. Build the estimation panels from the staged genuine panels: write the genuine columns into the pipeline's DataRaw_<C>_glob.json contract (RAW level/percent space → apply each spec.json transform), with honest NaN ragged tails (NO WEO extension), and the ROW* columns left for the loop. Recompute SFA_CN app-side.
  4. Re-estimate the WHOLE GVAR (export_bvar_gvar24_loop.m, 1000 draws) — every bloc re-estimates because dropping RU + re-sourcing 5 blocs changes every partner's stars. ~8–16 h unattended. The loop's buildstars renormalises trade weights over partners present each quarter (a 2024-Q1-ending bloc just drops from later star sums).
  5. Re-export per-bloc JSON contract into app/public/data/bvar/<C>/ (~5 min); delete RU dir.
  6. Coupling regen (MANDATORY) — precompute_gvar_coupling.mjs; new global modelSig; ~20–25 min. Confirm US topology first.
  7. Verify — artifact==live (gvar-coupling-artifact.test.ts), convergence/coherence, deterministic-mean + gt.m canary, CF==iterative <1e-4, spot-check the 5 re-sourced + RU-absent topology. Expect IN (and any 2024–2025-ending bloc) to be prior-dominated at the tail — flag, not fail.
  8. Ship only after green-gate + diff review.

End-to-end ≈ 2–3.5 calendar days, dominated by (i) the human-check resolution / national re-source decision and (ii) the 8–16 h re-estimation floor — NOT the coupling regen.
